But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kotani Kofun | image = [[File:Kotani Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = | part_of = | location = [[Toriya-cho]], [[Kashihara]], [[Nara Prefecture]] | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 28 | coordinates = 29.03 | coordinates = 135 | coordinates = 47 | coordinates = 5.52 | omitted = JP-29 | type = Unknown | area = Length: 30 m
Height: 8 m | burial = Double-winged [[yokoana-style stone chamber]] (Iwayayama style)
(Contains a hewn [[house-shaped sarcophagus]]) | Excavated Items = | Satellite Tombs = | built = [[7th century]] | Buried Figure = | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = Designated historic site of Nara Prefecture: "Kotani Kofun" | Tangible Cultural Property = | notes = | map = Japan Nara | type = | map_dot_label = Kotani Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kotani Kofun''' (小谷古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Kashihara|ja|橿原市}}, [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is designation1 as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == The Kotani Kofun is situated on the southern slope of a ridge extending northeast from Kaibukiyama at the southern edge of the [[Nara Basin]]Historic Site Explanation Board.. The mound is part of a cluster of eight kofun in the area, positioned at the easternmost point of the group. The mound has suffered significant erosion, and no archaeological excavations have been conducted. The mound's type is uncertain but is hypothesized to be either circular or square. It measures [[Category:pp error]]approximately 30 meters in length and 8 meters in height. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} with double sidewalls, opening to the south. The stone chamber is constructed with well-cut granite blocks and features a sophisticated design similar to the {{ill|Iwayayama Kofun|ja|岩屋山古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka|ja|明日香村}}, making it an example of the "Iwayayama-style stone chamber." Within the chamber lies a {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} carved from [[Hyogo Prefecture]]'s Tatsuyama Stone (stratified hyaloclastite)Tatsuyama Stone has been identified as rhyolitic stratified hyaloclastite (water-cooled pyroclastic rock) based on recent studies. See [https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html Tatsuyama Stone (Hyogo Prefectural Website)] for details.. The grave goods have not been confirmed due to the lack of excavation. The mound dates to the [[7th century]] during the late [[Kofun period]]. It is recognized as one of the representative terminal period kofun in Nara Prefecture, featuring a stone chamber comparable in map_size to those used for royal tombs. While the identity of the interred is unknown, the burial is speculated to belong to an influential aristocratic family, possibly of royal lineage. In the [[Edo period]], it was suggested to be the tomb of [[Empress Saimei]]. The site was designation1 as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site in [[1975]] (Showa 50). Currently, access to the interior of the stone chamber is restricted. Nearby is the Kotani East Kofun (whose stone chamber remains unopened), and it has been proposed that the two kofun form a twin Kofun pairKunihiko Kawakami, *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Fusosha, 2004, 11. 70-72.. == burial == [[File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird's-eye view of the stone chamber.}}]] [[File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Detailed layout of the stone chamber.}}]] [[File:Kotani Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the carved sarcophagus.}}]] The burial facility comprises a double sidewall corridor-style stone chamber, opening to the south. Its area are as follows: * '''Total length:''' A11roximately 11.6 meters * '''Main chamber:''' Length ~5 meters, Width ~2.8 meters, Height ~2.7 meters * '''Entrance corridor:''' Length ~6.45 meters, Width ~1.9 meters, Height ~1.8 meters The chamber is constructed from large, cut granite blocks with meticulous craftsmanship. Both the rear and side walls of the main chamber consist of two layers, and the seams are sealed with plaster. A single large stone serves as the ceiling. The main chamber floor is elevated one step above the corridor. Inside the main chamber lies a house-shaped sarcophagus carved from Tatsuyama Stone[https://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/kotani.html Kotani Kofun] (Kashihara City "Kashihara Visit Navi").. The lid, almost semicylindrical in type, lacks suspension lugs, indicating a newer style within house-shaped sarcophagi. The sarcophagus measures 2.4 meters in length, 1.16 meters in width, and 0.82 meters in height. The chamber shares features with the Iwayayama Kofun, classified as "Iwayayama-style." While the main chambers are similar in size, the corridor of Kotani Kofun is shorter. The stone chamber is also nearly identical in design to the {{ill|Minezuka Kofun|ja|峯塚古墳}} in {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市}}, suggesting a shift from local clan control of stonework to central administration under the imperial court during the terminal kofun periodKunihiko Kawakami, *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Fusosha, 2004, 11. 66-69.. Minezuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Reference: Bird's-eye view of the Minezuka Kofun stone chamber Kotani Kofun, genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the rear wall) Kotani Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Carved house-shaped sarcophagus inside the main chamber Kotani Kofun, genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Kotani Kofun, sendou-2-2.jpg|Entrance corridor (view toward the entrance) Kotani Kofun, sendou-1-2.jpg|Entrance corridor (view toward the main chamber) Kotani Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Entrance == Historic Site == === Nara Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Kotani Kofun: Designated on March 31, 1975 (Showa 50){{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf Nara Prefecture Cultural Property List]}} (Nara Prefectural Website).. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical site explanation board (installed by the Kashihara City Board of Education). * {{Cite book|year=1981|chapter=Kotani Kofun|title=Nara Prefecture Place names|series=[[Japanese Historical Place name Series]] 30|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Kotani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} == External Links == In the [[Edo period]], it was suggested to be the tomb of [[Empress Saimei]]. The site was designation1 as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site in [[1975]] (Showa 50). Currently, access to the interior of the stone chamber is restricted. Nearby is the Kotani East Kofun (whose stone chamber remains unopened), and it has been proposed that the two kofun form a twin Kofun pairKunihiko Kawakami, *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Fusosha, 2004, 11. 70-72.. == burial == [[File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird's-eye view of the stone chamber.}}]] [[File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Detailed layout of the stone chamber.}}]] [[File:Kotani Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the carved sarcophagus.}}]] The burial facility comprises a double sidewall corridor-style stone chamber, opening to the south. Its area are as follows: * '''Total length:''' A11roximately 11.6 meters * '''Main chamber:''' Length ~5 meters, Width ~2.8 meters, Height ~2.7 meters * '''Entrance corridor:''' Length ~6.45 meters, Width ~1.9 meters, Height ~1.8 meters The chamber is constructed from large, cut granite blocks with meticulous craftsmanship. Both the rear and side walls of the main chamber consist of two layers, and the seams are sealed with plaster. A single large stone serves as the ceiling. The main chamber floor is elevated one step above the corridor. Inside the main chamber lies a house-shaped sarcophagus carved from Tatsuyama Stone[https://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/kotani.html Kotani Kofun] (Kashihara City "Kashihara Visit Navi").. The lid, almost semicylindrical in type, lacks suspension lugs, indicating a newer style within house-shaped sarcophagi. The sarcophagus measures 2.4 meters in length, 1.16 meters in width, and 0.82 meters in height. The chamber shares features with the Iwayayama Kofun, classified as "Iwayayama-style." While the main chambers are similar in size, the corridor of Kotani Kofun is shorter. The stone chamber is also nearly identical in design to the {{ill|Minezuka Kofun|ja|峯塚古墳}} in {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市}}, suggesting a shift from local clan control of stonework to central administration under the imperial court during the terminal kofun periodKunihiko Kawakami, *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Fusosha, 2004, 11. 66-69.. Minezuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Reference: Bird's-eye view of the Minezuka Kofun stone chamber Kotani Kofun, genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the rear wall) Kotani Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Carved house-shaped sarcophagus inside the main chamber Kotani Kofun, genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Kotani Kofun, sendou-2-2.jpg|Entrance corridor (view toward the entrance) Kotani Kofun, sendou-1-2.jpg|Entrance corridor (view toward the main chamber) Kotani Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Entrance == Historic Site == === Nara Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Kotani Kofun: Designated on March 31, 1975 (Showa 50){{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf Nara Prefecture Cultural Property List]}} (Nara Prefectural Website).. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical site explanation board (installed by the Kashihara City Board of Education). * {{Cite book|year=1981|chapter=Kotani Kofun|title=Nara Prefecture Place names|series=[[Japanese Historical Place name Series]] 30|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Kotani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kotani Kofun}} * [https://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/kotani.html Kotani Kofun] - Kashihara City "Kashihara Visit Navi" {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:pp error]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashihara]] [[Category:Nara Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Kodani Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:completion tier 3]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Kodani Kofun]] [[ja:小谷古墳]] [[sn:odani Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kodani Kofun}}

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kotani Kofun | image = [[File:Kotani Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = | part_of = | location = [[Toriya-cho]], [[Kashihara]], [[Nara Prefecture]] | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 28 | coordinates = 29.03 | coordinates = 135 | coordinates = 47 | coordinates = 5.52 | omitted = JP-29 | type = Unknown | area = Length: 30 m
Height: 8 m | burial = Double-winged [[yokoana-style stone chamber]] (Iwayayama style)
(Contains a hewn [[house-shaped sarcophagus]]) | Excavated Items = | Satellite Tombs = | built = [[7th century]] | Buried Figure = | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = Designated historic site of Nara Prefecture: "Kotani Kofun" | Tangible Cultural Property = | notes = | map = Japan Nara | type = | map_dot_label = Kotani Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kotani Kofun''' (小谷古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Kashihara|ja|橿原市|12=draft}}, [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is designation1 as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == The Kotani Kofun is situated on the southern slope of a ridge extending northeast from Kaibukiyama at the southern edge of the [[Nara Basin]]Historic Site Explanation Board.. The mound is part of a cluster of eight kofun in the area, positioned at the easternmost point of the group. The mound has suffered significant erosion, and no archaeological excavations have been conducted. The mound's type is uncertain but is hypothesized to be either circular or square. It measures approximately 30 meters in length and 8 meters in height. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} with double sidewalls, opening to the south. The stone chamber is constructed with well-cut granite blocks and features a sophisticated design similar to the {{ill|Iwayayama Kofun|ja|岩屋山古墳|12=draft}} in {{ill|Asuka|ja|明日香村|12=draft}}, making it an example of the "Iwayayama-style stone chamber." Within the chamber lies a {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=draft}} carved from [[Hyogo Prefecture]]'s Tatsuyama Stone (stratified hyaloclastite)Tatsuyama Stone has been identified as rhyolitic stratified hyaloclastite (water-cooled pyroclastic rock) based on recent studies. See [https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html Tatsuyama Stone (Hyogo Prefectural Website)] for details.. The grave goods have not been confirmed due to the lack of excavation. The mound dates to the [[7th century]] during the late [[Kofun period]]. It is recognized as one of the representative terminal period kofun in Nara Prefecture, featuring a stone chamber comparable in map_size to those used for royal tombs. While the identity of the interred is unknown, the burial is speculated to belong to an influential aristocratic family, possibly of royal lineage. In the [[Edo period]], it was suggested to be the tomb of [[Empress Saimei]]. The site was designation1 as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site in [[1975]] (Showa 50). Currently, access to the interior of the stone chamber is restricted. Nearby is the Kotani East Kofun (whose stone chamber remains unopened), and it has been proposed that the two kofun form a twin Kofun pairKunihiko Kawakami, *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Fusosha, 2004, 11. 70-72.. == burial == [[File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird's-eye view of the stone chamber.}}]] [[File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Detailed layout of the stone chamber.}}]] [[File:Kotani Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the carved sarcophagus.}}]] The burial facility comprises a double sidewall corridor-style stone chamber, opening to the south. Its area are as follows: * '''Total length:''' A11roximately 11.6 meters * '''Main chamber:''' Length ~5 meters, Width ~2.8 meters, Height ~2.7 meters * '''Entrance corridor:''' Length ~6.45 meters, Width ~1.9 meters, Height ~1.8 meters The chamber is constructed from large, cut granite blocks with meticulous craftsmanship. Both the rear and side walls of the main chamber consist of two layers, and the seams are sealed with plaster. A single large stone serves as the ceiling. The main chamber floor is elevated one step above the corridor. Inside the main chamber lies a house-shaped sarcophagus carved from Tatsuyama Stone[https://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/kotani.html Kotani Kofun] (Kashihara City "Kashihara Visit Navi").. The lid, almost semicylindrical in type, lacks suspension lugs, indicating a newer style within house-shaped sarcophagi. The sarcophagus measures 2.4 meters in length, 1.16 meters in width, and 0.82 meters in height. The chamber shares features with the Iwayayama Kofun, classified as "Iwayayama-style." While the main chambers are similar in size, the corridor of Kotani Kofun is shorter. The stone chamber is also nearly identical in design to the {{ill|Minezuka Kofun|ja|峯塚古墳|12=draft}} in {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市|12=draft}}, suggesting a shift from local clan control of stonework to central administration under the imperial court during the terminal kofun periodKunihiko Kawakami, *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Fusosha, 2004, 11. 66-69.. Minezuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Reference: Bird's-eye view of the Minezuka Kofun stone chamber Kotani Kofun, genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the rear wall) Kotani Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Carved house-shaped sarcophagus inside the main chamber Kotani Kofun, genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Kotani Kofun, sendou-2-2.jpg|Entrance corridor (view toward the entrance) Kotani Kofun, sendou-1-2.jpg|Entrance corridor (view toward the main chamber) Kotani Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Entrance == Historic Site == === Nara Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Kotani Kofun: Designated on March 31, 1975 (Showa 50){{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf Nara Prefecture Cultural Property List]}} (Nara Prefectural Website).. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical site explanation board (installed by the Kashihara City Board of Education). * {{Cite book|year=1981|chapter=Kotani Kofun|title=Nara Prefecture Place names|series=[[Japanese Historical Place name Series]] 30|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Kotani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kotani Kofun}} * [https://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/kotani.html Kotani Kofun] - Kashihara City "Kashihara Visit Navi" {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:pp error]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashihara]] [[Category:Nara Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Kodani Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:completion tier 3]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Kodani Kofun]] [[ja:小谷古墳]] [[sn:odani Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kodani Kofun}}

The lid, almost semicylindrical in type, lacks suspension lugs, indicating a newer style within house-shaped sarcophagi. The sarcophagus measures 2.4 meters in length, 1.16 meters in width, and 0.82 meters in height. The chamber shares features with the Iwayayama Kofun, classified as "Iwayayama-style." While the main chambers are similar in size, the corridor of Kotani Kofun is shorter. The stone chamber is also nearly identical in design to the {{ill|Minezuka Kofun|ja|峯塚古墳|12=draft}} in {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市|12=draft}}, suggesting a shift from local clan control of stonework to central administration under the imperial court during the terminal kofun periodKunihiko Kawakami, *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Fusosha, 2004, 11. 66-69.. revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kotani Kofun | image = [[File:Kotani Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = | part_of = | location = [[Toriya-cho]], [[Kashihara]], [[Nara Prefecture]] | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 28 | coordinates = 29.03 | coordinates = 135 | coordinates = 47 | coordinates = 5.52 | omitted = JP-29 | type = Unknown | area = Length: 30 m
Height: 8 m | burial = Double-winged [[yokoana-style stone chamber]] (Iwayayama style)
(Contains a hewn [[house-shaped sarcophagus]]) | Excavated Items = | Satellite Tombs = | built = [[7th century]] | Buried Figure = | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = Designated historic site of Nara Prefecture: "Kotani Kofun" | Tangible Cultural Property = | notes = | map = Japan Nara | type = | map_dot_label = Kotani Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kotani Kofun''' (小谷古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Kashihara|ja|橿原市|12=draft}}, [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is designation1 as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == The Kotani Kofun is situated on the southern slope of a ridge extending northeast from Kaibukiyama at the southern edge of the [[Nara Basin]]Historic Site Explanation Board.. The mound is part of a cluster of eight kofun in the area, positioned at the easternmost point of the group. The mound has suffered significant erosion, and no archaeological excavations have been conducted. The mound's type is uncertain but is hypothesized to be either circular or square. It measures approximately 30 meters in length and 8 meters in height. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} with double sidewalls, opening to the south. The stone chamber is constructed with well-cut granite blocks and features a sophisticated design similar to the {{ill|Iwayayama Kofun|ja|岩屋山古墳|12=draft}} in {{ill|Asuka|ja|明日香村|12=draft}}, making it an example of the "Iwayayama-style stone chamber." Within the chamber lies a {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=draft}} carved from [[Hyogo Prefecture]]'s Tatsuyama Stone (stratified hyaloclastite)Tatsuyama Stone has been identified as rhyolitic stratified hyaloclastite (water-cooled pyroclastic rock) based on recent studies. See [https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html Tatsuyama Stone (Hyogo Prefectural Website)] for details.. The grave goods have not been confirmed due to the lack of excavation. The mound dates to the [[7th century]] during the late [[Kofun period]]. It is recognized as one of the representative terminal period kofun in Nara Prefecture, featuring a stone chamber comparable in map_size to those used for royal tombs. While the identity of the interred is unknown, the burial is speculated to belong to an influential aristocratic family, possibly of royal lineage. In the [[Edo period]], it was suggested to be the tomb of [[Empress Saimei]]. The site was designation1 as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site in [[1975]] (Showa 50). Currently, access to the interior of the stone chamber is restricted. Nearby is the Kotani East Kofun (whose stone chamber remains unopened), and it has been proposed that the two kofun form a twin Kofun pairKunihiko Kawakami, *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Fusosha, 2004, 11. 70-72.. == burial == [[File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird's-eye view of the stone chamber.}}]] [[File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Detailed layout of the stone chamber.}}]] [[File:Kotani Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the carved sarcophagus.}}]] The burial facility comprises a double sidewall corridor-style stone chamber, opening to the south. Its area are as follows: * '''Total length:''' A11roximately 11.6 meters * '''Main chamber:''' Length ~5 meters, Width ~2.8 meters, Height ~2.7 meters * '''Entrance corridor:''' Length ~6.45 meters, Width ~1.9 meters, Height ~1.8 meters The chamber is constructed from large, cut granite blocks with meticulous craftsmanship. Both the rear and side walls of the main chamber consist of two layers, and the seams are sealed with plaster. A single large stone serves as the ceiling. The main chamber floor is elevated one step above the corridor. Inside the main chamber lies a house-shaped sarcophagus carved from Tatsuyama Stone[https://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/kotani.html Kotani Kofun] (Kashihara City "Kashihara Visit Navi").. The lid, almost semicylindrical in type, lacks suspension lugs, indicating a newer style within house-shaped sarcophagi. The sarcophagus measures 2.4 meters in length, 1.16 meters in width, and 0.82 meters in height. The chamber shares features with the Iwayayama Kofun, classified as "Iwayayama-style." While the main chambers are similar in size, the corridor of Kotani Kofun is shorter. The stone chamber is also nearly identical in design to the {{ill|Minezuka Kofun|ja|峯塚古墳|12=draft}} in {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市|12=draft}}, suggesting a shift from local clan control of stonework to central administration under the imperial court during the terminal kofun periodKunihiko Kawakami, *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Fusosha, 2004, 11. 66-69.. Minezuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Reference: Bird's-eye view of the Minezuka Kofun stone chamber Kotani Kofun, genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the rear wall) Kotani Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Carved house-shaped sarcophagus inside the main chamber Kotani Kofun, genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Kotani Kofun, sendou-2-2.jpg|Entrance corridor (view toward the entrance) Kotani Kofun, sendou-1-2.jpg|Entrance corridor (view toward the main chamber) Kotani Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Entrance == Historic Site == === Nara Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Kotani Kofun: Designated on March 31, 1975 (Showa 50){{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf Nara Prefecture Cultural Property List]}} (Nara Prefectural Website).. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical site explanation board (installed by the Kashihara City Board of Education). * {{Cite book|year=1981|chapter=Kotani Kofun|title=Nara Prefecture Place names|series=[[Japanese Historical Place name Series]] 30|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Kotani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kotani Kofun}} * [https://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/kotani.html Kotani Kofun] - Kashihara City "Kashihara Visit Navi" {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:pp error]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashihara]] [[Category:Nara Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Kodani Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:completion tier 3]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Kodani Kofun]] [[ja:小谷古墳]] [[sn:odani Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kodani Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]]

Entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = | part_of = | location = [[Toriya-cho]], [[Kashihara]], [[Nara Prefecture]] | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 28 | coordinates = 29.03 | coordinates = 135 | coordinates = 47 | coordinates = 5.52 | omitted = JP-29 | type = Unknown | area = Length: 30 m
Height: 8 m | burial = Double-winged [[yokoana-style stone chamber]] (Iwayayama style)
(Contains a hewn [[house-shaped sarcophagus]]) | Excavated Items = | Satellite Tombs = | built = [[7th century]] | Buried Figure = | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = Designated historic site of Nara Prefecture: "Kotani Kofun" | Tangible Cultural Property = | notes = | map = Japan Nara | type = | map_dot_label = Kotani Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kotani Kofun''' (小谷古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Kashihara|ja|橿原市|12=draft}}, [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is designation1 as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == The Kotani Kofun is situated on the southern slope of a ridge extending northeast from Kaibukiyama at the southern edge of the [[Nara Basin]]Historic Site Explanation Board.. The mound is part of a cluster of eight kofun in the area, positioned at the easternmost point of the group. The mound has suffered significant erosion, and no archaeological excavations have been conducted. The mound's type is uncertain but is hypothesized to be either circular or square. It measures approximately 30 meters in length and 8 meters in height. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} with double sidewalls, opening to the south. The stone chamber is constructed with well-cut granite blocks and features a sophisticated design similar to the {{ill|Iwayayama Kofun|ja|岩屋山古墳|12=draft}} in {{ill|Asuka|ja|明日香村|12=draft}}, making it an example of the "Iwayayama-style stone chamber." Within the chamber lies a {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=draft}} carved from [[Hyogo Prefecture]]'s Tatsuyama Stone (stratified hyaloclastite)Tatsuyama Stone has been identified as rhyolitic stratified hyaloclastite (water-cooled pyroclastic rock) based on recent studies. See [https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html Tatsuyama Stone (Hyogo Prefectural Website)] for details.. The grave goods have not been confirmed due to the lack of excavation. The mound dates to the [[7th century]] during the late [[Kofun period]]. It is recognized as one of the representative terminal period kofun in Nara Prefecture, featuring a stone chamber comparable in map_size to those used for royal tombs. While the identity of the interred is unknown, the burial is speculated to belong to an influential aristocratic family, possibly of royal lineage. In the [[Edo period]], it was suggested to be the tomb of [[Empress Saimei]]. The site was designation1 as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site in [[1975]] (Showa 50). Currently, access to the interior of the stone chamber is restricted. Nearby is the Kotani East Kofun (whose stone chamber remains unopened), and it has been proposed that the two kofun form a twin Kofun pairKunihiko Kawakami, *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Fusosha, 2004, 11. 70-72.. == burial == [[File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird's-eye view of the stone chamber.}}]] [[File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Detailed layout of the stone chamber.}}]] [[File:Kotani Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the carved sarcophagus.}}]] The burial facility comprises a double sidewall corridor-style stone chamber, opening to the south. Its area are as follows: * '''Total length:''' A11roximately 11.6 meters * '''Main chamber:''' Length ~5 meters, Width ~2.8 meters, Height ~2.7 meters * '''Entrance corridor:''' Length ~6.45 meters, Width ~1.9 meters, Height ~1.8 meters The chamber is constructed from large, cut granite blocks with meticulous craftsmanship. Both the rear and side walls of the main chamber consist of two layers, and the seams are sealed with plaster. A single large stone serves as the ceiling. The main chamber floor is elevated one step above the corridor. Inside the main chamber lies a house-shaped sarcophagus carved from Tatsuyama Stone[https://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/kotani.html Kotani Kofun] (Kashihara City "Kashihara Visit Navi").. The lid, almost semicylindrical in type, lacks suspension lugs, indicating a newer style within house-shaped sarcophagi. The sarcophagus measures 2.4 meters in length, 1.16 meters in width, and 0.82 meters in height. The chamber shares features with the Iwayayama Kofun, classified as "Iwayayama-style." While the main chambers are similar in size, the corridor of Kotani Kofun is shorter. The stone chamber is also nearly identical in design to the {{ill|Minezuka Kofun|ja|峯塚古墳|12=draft}} in {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市|12=draft}}, suggesting a shift from local clan control of stonework to central administration under the imperial court during the terminal kofun periodKunihiko Kawakami, *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Fusosha, 2004, 11. 66-69.. Minezuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Reference: Bird's-eye view of the Minezuka Kofun stone chamber Kotani Kofun, genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the rear wall) Kotani Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Carved house-shaped sarcophagus inside the main chamber Kotani Kofun, genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Kotani Kofun, sendou-2-2.jpg|Entrance corridor (view toward the entrance) Kotani Kofun, sendou-1-2.jpg|Entrance corridor (view toward the main chamber) Kotani Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Entrance == Historic Site == === Nara Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Kotani Kofun: Designated on March 31, 1975 (Showa 50){{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf Nara Prefecture Cultural Property List]}} (Nara Prefectural Website).. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical site explanation board (installed by the Kashihara City Board of Education). * {{Cite book|year=1981|chapter=Kotani Kofun|title=Nara Prefecture Place names|series=[[Japanese Historical Place name Series]] 30|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Kotani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kotani Kofun}} * [https://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/kotani.html Kotani Kofun] - {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kotani Kofun | image = [[File:Kotani Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = | part_of = | location = [[Toriya-cho]], [[Kashihara]], [[Nara Prefecture]] | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 28 | coordinates = 29.03 | coordinates = 135 | coordinates = 47 | coordinates = 5.52 | omitted = JP-29 | type = Unknown | area = Length: 30 m
Height: 8 m | burial = Double-winged [[yokoana-style stone chamber]] (Iwayayama style)
(Contains a hewn [[house-shaped sarcophagus]]) | Excavated Items = | Satellite Tombs = | built = [[7th century]] | Buried Figure = | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = Designated historic site of Nara Prefecture: "Kotani Kofun" | Tangible Cultural Property = | notes = | map = Japan Nara | type = | map_dot_label = Kotani Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kotani Kofun''' (小谷古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Kashihara|ja|橿原市|12=draft}}, [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is designation1 as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == The Kotani Kofun is situated on the southern slope of a ridge extending northeast from Kaibukiyama at the southern edge of the [[Nara Basin]]Historic Site Explanation Board.. The mound is part of a cluster of eight kofun in the area, positioned at the easternmost point of the group. The mound has suffered significant erosion, and no archaeological excavations have been conducted. The mound's type is uncertain but is hypothesized to be either circular or square. It measures approximately 30 meters in length and 8 meters in height. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} with double sidewalls, opening to the south. The stone chamber is constructed with well-cut granite blocks and features a sophisticated design similar to the {{ill|Iwayayama Kofun|ja|岩屋山古墳|12=draft}} in {{ill|Asuka|ja|明日香村|12=draft}}, making it an example of the "Iwayayama-style stone chamber." Within the chamber lies a {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=draft}} carved from [[Hyogo Prefecture]]'s Tatsuyama Stone (stratified hyaloclastite)Tatsuyama Stone has been identified as rhyolitic stratified hyaloclastite (water-cooled pyroclastic rock) based on recent studies. See [https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html Tatsuyama Stone (Hyogo Prefectural Website)] for details.. The grave goods have not been confirmed due to the lack of excavation. The mound dates to the [[7th century]] during the late [[Kofun period]]. It is recognized as one of the representative terminal period kofun in Nara Prefecture, featuring a stone chamber comparable in map_size to those used for royal tombs. While the identity of the interred is unknown, the burial is speculated to belong to an influential aristocratic family, possibly of royal lineage. In the [[Edo period]], it was suggested to be the tomb of [[Empress Saimei]]. The site was designation1 as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site in [[1975]] (Showa 50). Currently, access to the interior of the stone chamber is restricted. Nearby is the Kotani East Kofun (whose stone chamber remains unopened), and it has been proposed that the two kofun form a twin Kofun pairKunihiko Kawakami, *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Fusosha, 2004, 11. 70-72.. == burial == [[File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird's-eye view of the stone chamber.}}]] [[File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Detailed layout of the stone chamber.}}]] [[File:Kotani Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the carved sarcophagus.}}]] The burial facility comprises a double sidewall corridor-style stone chamber, opening to the south. Its area are as follows: * '''Total length:''' A11roximately 11.6 meters * '''Main chamber:''' Length ~5 meters, Width ~2.8 meters, Height ~2.7 meters * '''Entrance corridor:''' Length ~6.45 meters, Width ~1.9 meters, Height ~1.8 meters The chamber is constructed from large, cut granite blocks with meticulous craftsmanship. Both the rear and side walls of the main chamber consist of two layers, and the seams are sealed with plaster. A single large stone serves as the ceiling. The main chamber floor is elevated one step above the corridor. Inside the main chamber lies a house-shaped sarcophagus carved from Tatsuyama Stone[https://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/kotani.html Kotani Kofun] (Kashihara City "Kashihara Visit Navi").. The lid, almost semicylindrical in type, lacks suspension lugs, indicating a newer style within house-shaped sarcophagi. The sarcophagus measures 2.4 meters in length, 1.16 meters in width, and 0.82 meters in height. The chamber shares features with the Iwayayama Kofun, classified as "Iwayayama-style." While the main chambers are similar in size, the corridor of Kotani Kofun is shorter. The stone chamber is also nearly identical in design to the {{ill|Minezuka Kofun|ja|峯塚古墳|12=draft}} in {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市|12=draft}}, suggesting a shift from local clan control of stonework to central administration under the imperial court during the terminal kofun periodKunihiko Kawakami, *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Fusosha, 2004, 11. 66-69.. Minezuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Reference: Bird's-eye view of the Minezuka Kofun stone chamber Kotani Kofun, genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the rear wall) Kotani Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Carved house-shaped sarcophagus inside the main chamber Kotani Kofun, genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Kotani Kofun, sendou-2-2.jpg|Entrance corridor (view toward the entrance) Kotani Kofun, sendou-1-2.jpg|Entrance corridor (view toward the main chamber) Kotani Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Entrance == Historic Site == === Nara Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Kotani Kofun: Designated on March 31, 1975 (Showa 50){{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf Nara Prefecture Cultural Property List]}} (Nara Prefectural Website).. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical site explanation board (installed by the Kashihara City Board of Education). * {{Cite book|year=1981|chapter=Kotani Kofun|title=Nara Prefecture Place names|series=[[Japanese Historical Place name Series]] 30|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Kotani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kotani Kofun}} * [https://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/kotani.html Kotani Kofun] - Entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = | part_of = | location = [[Toriya-cho]], [[Kashihara]], [[Nara Prefecture]] | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 28 | coordinates = 29.03 | coordinates = 135 | coordinates = 47 | coordinates = 5.52 | omitted = JP-29 | type = Unknown | area = Length: 30 m
Height: 8 m | burial = Double-winged [[yokoana-style stone chamber]] (Iwayayama style)
(Contains a hewn [[house-shaped sarcophagus]]) | Excavated Items = | Satellite Tombs = | built = [[7th century]] | Buried Figure = | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = Designated historic site of Nara Prefecture: "Kotani Kofun" | Tangible Cultural Property = | notes = | map = Japan Nara | type = | map_dot_label = Kotani Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kotani Kofun''' (小谷古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Kashihara|ja|橿原市|12=draft}}, [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is designation1 as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == The Kotani Kofun is situated on the southern slope of a ridge extending northeast from Kaibukiyama at the southern edge of the [[Nara Basin]]Historic Site Explanation Board.. The mound is part of a cluster of eight kofun in the area, positioned at the easternmost point of the group. The mound has suffered significant erosion, and no archaeological excavations have been conducted. The mound's type is uncertain but is hypothesized to be either circular or square. It measures approximately 30 meters in length and 8 meters in height. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} with double sidewalls, opening to the south. The stone chamber is constructed with well-cut granite blocks and features a sophisticated design similar to the {{ill|Iwayayama Kofun|ja|岩屋山古墳|12=draft}} in {{ill|Asuka|ja|明日香村|12=draft}}, making it an example of the "Iwayayama-style stone chamber." Within the chamber lies a {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=draft}} carved from [[Hyogo Prefecture]]'s Tatsuyama Stone (stratified hyaloclastite)Tatsuyama Stone has been identified as rhyolitic stratified hyaloclastite (water-cooled pyroclastic rock) based on recent studies. See [https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html Tatsuyama Stone (Hyogo Prefectural Website)] for details.. The grave goods have not been confirmed due to the lack of excavation. The mound dates to the [[7th century]] during the late [[Kofun period]]. It is recognized as one of the representative terminal period kofun in Nara Prefecture, featuring a stone chamber comparable in map_size to those used for royal tombs. While the identity of the interred is unknown, the burial is speculated to belong to an influential aristocratic family, possibly of royal lineage. In the [[Edo period]], it was suggested to be the tomb of [[Empress Saimei]]. The site was designation1 as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site in [[1975]] (Showa 50). Currently, access to the interior of the stone chamber is restricted. Nearby is the Kotani East Kofun (whose stone chamber remains unopened), and it has been proposed that the two kofun form a twin Kofun pairKunihiko Kawakami, *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Fusosha, 2004, 11. 70-72.. == burial == [[File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird's-eye view of the stone chamber.}}]] [[File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Detailed layout of the stone chamber.}}]] [[File:Kotani Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the carved sarcophagus.}}]] The burial facility comprises a double sidewall corridor-style stone chamber, opening to the south. Its area are as follows: * '''Total length:''' A11roximately 11.6 meters * '''Main chamber:''' Length ~5 meters, Width ~2.8 meters, Height ~2.7 meters * '''Entrance corridor:''' Length ~6.45 meters, Width ~1.9 meters, Height ~1.8 meters The chamber is constructed from large, cut granite blocks with meticulous craftsmanship. Both the rear and side walls of the main chamber consist of two layers, and the seams are sealed with plaster. A single large stone serves as the ceiling. The main chamber floor is elevated one step above the corridor. Inside the main chamber lies a house-shaped sarcophagus carved from Tatsuyama Stone[https://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/kotani.html Kotani Kofun] (Kashihara City "Kashihara Visit Navi").. The lid, almost semicylindrical in type, lacks suspension lugs, indicating a newer style within house-shaped sarcophagi. The sarcophagus measures 2.4 meters in length, 1.16 meters in width, and 0.82 meters in height. The chamber shares features with the Iwayayama Kofun, classified as "Iwayayama-style." While the main chambers are similar in size, the corridor of Kotani Kofun is shorter. The stone chamber is also nearly identical in design to the {{ill|Minezuka Kofun|ja|峯塚古墳|12=draft}} in {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市|12=draft}}, suggesting a shift from local clan control of stonework to central administration under the imperial court during the terminal kofun periodKunihiko Kawakami, *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Fusosha, 2004, 11. 66-69.. Minezuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Reference: Bird's-eye view of the Minezuka Kofun stone chamber Kotani Kofun, genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the rear wall) Kotani Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Carved house-shaped sarcophagus inside the main chamber Kotani Kofun, genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Kotani Kofun, sendou-2-2.jpg|Entrance corridor (view toward the entrance) Kotani Kofun, sendou-1-2.jpg|Entrance corridor (view toward the main chamber) Kotani Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Entrance == Historic Site == === Nara Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Kotani Kofun: Designated on March 31, 1975 (Showa 50){{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf Nara Prefecture Cultural Property List]}} (Nara Prefectural Website).. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical site explanation board (installed by the Kashihara City Board of Education). * {{Cite book|year=1981|chapter=Kotani Kofun|title=Nara Prefecture Place names|series=[[Japanese Historical Place name Series]] 30|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Kotani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kotani Kofun}} * [https://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/kotani.html Kotani Kofun] - revision.comment: Bot: single‑shot reformat + ill→|12=draft + draft categories + iws + DEFAULTSORT + move HTML comments + meta‑cat check
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kotani Kofun | image = [[File:Kotani Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = | part_of = | location = [[Toriya-cho]], [[Kashihara]], [[Nara Prefecture]] | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 28 | coordinates = 29.03 | coordinates = 135 | coordinates = 47 | coordinates = 5.52 | omitted = JP-29 | type = Unknown | area = Length: 30 m
Height: 8 m | burial = Double-winged [[yokoana-style stone chamber]] (Iwayayama style)
(Contains a hewn [[house-shaped sarcophagus]]) | Excavated Items = | Satellite Tombs = | built = [[7th century]] | Buried Figure = | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = Designated historic site of Nara Prefecture: "Kotani Kofun" | Tangible Cultural Property = | notes = | map = Japan Nara | type = | map_dot_label = Kotani Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kotani Kofun''' (小谷古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Kashihara|ja|橿原市|12=draft}}, [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is designation1 as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == The Kotani Kofun is situated on the southern slope of a ridge extending northeast from Kaibukiyama at the southern edge of the [[Nara Basin]]Historic Site Explanation Board.. The mound is part of a cluster of eight kofun in the area, positioned at the easternmost point of the group. The mound has suffered significant erosion, and no archaeological excavations have been conducted. The mound's type is uncertain but is hypothesized to be either circular or square. It measures approximately 30 meters in length and 8 meters in height. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} with double sidewalls, opening to the south. The stone chamber is constructed with well-cut granite blocks and features a sophisticated design similar to the {{ill|Iwayayama Kofun|ja|岩屋山古墳|12=draft}} in {{ill|Asuka|ja|明日香村|12=draft}}, making it an example of the "Iwayayama-style stone chamber." Within the chamber lies a {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=draft}} carved from [[Hyogo Prefecture]]'s Tatsuyama Stone (stratified hyaloclastite)Tatsuyama Stone has been identified as rhyolitic stratified hyaloclastite (water-cooled pyroclastic rock) based on recent studies. See [https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html Tatsuyama Stone (Hyogo Prefectural Website)] for details.. The grave goods have not been confirmed due to the lack of excavation. The mound dates to the [[7th century]] during the late [[Kofun period]]. It is recognized as one of the representative terminal period kofun in Nara Prefecture, featuring a stone chamber comparable in map_size to those used for royal tombs. While the identity of the interred is unknown, the burial is speculated to belong to an influential aristocratic family, possibly of royal lineage. In the [[Edo period]], it was suggested to be the tomb of [[Empress Saimei]]. The site was designation1 as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site in [[1975]] (Showa 50). Currently, access to the interior of the stone chamber is restricted. Nearby is the Kotani East Kofun (whose stone chamber remains unopened), and it has been proposed that the two kofun form a twin Kofun pairKunihiko Kawakami, *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Fusosha, 2004, 11. 70-72.. == burial == [[File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird's-eye view of the stone chamber.}}]] [[File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Detailed layout of the stone chamber.}}]] [[File:Kotani Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the carved sarcophagus.}}]] The burial facility comprises a double sidewall corridor-style stone chamber, opening to the south. Its area are as follows: * '''Total length:''' A11roximately 11.6 meters * '''Main chamber:''' Length ~5 meters, Width ~2.8 meters, Height ~2.7 meters * '''Entrance corridor:''' Length ~6.45 meters, Width ~1.9 meters, Height ~1.8 meters The chamber is constructed from large, cut granite blocks with meticulous craftsmanship. Both the rear and side walls of the main chamber consist of two layers, and the seams are sealed with plaster. A single large stone serves as the ceiling. The main chamber floor is elevated one step above the corridor. Inside the main chamber lies a house-shaped sarcophagus carved from Tatsuyama Stone[https://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/kotani.html Kotani Kofun] (Kashihara City "Kashihara Visit Navi").. The lid, almost semicylindrical in type, lacks suspension lugs, indicating a newer style within house-shaped sarcophagi. The sarcophagus measures 2.4 meters in length, 1.16 meters in width, and 0.82 meters in height. The chamber shares features with the Iwayayama Kofun, classified as "Iwayayama-style." While the main chambers are similar in size, the corridor of Kotani Kofun is shorter. The stone chamber is also nearly identical in design to the {{ill|Minezuka Kofun|ja|峯塚古墳|12=draft}} in {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市|12=draft}}, suggesting a shift from local clan control of stonework to central administration under the imperial court during the terminal kofun periodKunihiko Kawakami, *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Fusosha, 2004, 11. 66-69.. Minezuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Reference: Bird's-eye view of the Minezuka Kofun stone chamber Kotani Kofun, genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the rear wall) Kotani Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Carved house-shaped sarcophagus inside the main chamber Kotani Kofun, genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Kotani Kofun, sendou-2-2.jpg|Entrance corridor (view toward the entrance) Kotani Kofun, sendou-1-2.jpg|Entrance corridor (view toward the main chamber) Kotani Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Entrance == Historic Site == === Nara Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Kotani Kofun: Designated on March 31, 1975 (Showa 50){{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf Nara Prefecture Cultural Property List]}} (Nara Prefectural Website).. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical site explanation board (installed by the Kashihara City Board of Education). * {{Cite book|year=1981|chapter=Kotani Kofun|title=Nara Prefecture Place names|series=[[Japanese Historical Place name Series]] 30|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Kotani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kotani Kofun}} * [https://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/kotani.html Kotani Kofun] - Kashihara City "Kashihara Visit Navi" {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Kodani Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashihara]] [[Category:Nara Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Kodani Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] }} [[sn:Kodani Kofun]] [[ja:小谷古墳]] [[sn:odani Kofun]]

The lid, almost semicylindrical in type, lacks suspension lugs, indicating a newer style within house-shaped sarcophagi. The sarcophagus measures 2.4 meters in length, 1.16 meters in width, and 0.82 meters in height. The chamber shares features with the Iwayayama Kofun, classified as "Iwayayama-style." While the main chambers are similar in size, the corridor of Kotani Kofun is shorter. The stone chamber is also nearly identical in design to the {{ill|Minezuka Kofun|ja|峯塚古墳|12=draft}} in {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市|12=draft}}, suggesting a shift from local clan control of stonework to central administration under the imperial court during the terminal kofun periodKunihiko Kawakami, *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Fusosha, 2004, 11. 66-69.. Entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = | part_of = | location = [[Toriya-cho]], [[Kashihara]], [[Nara Prefecture]] | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 28 | coordinates = 29.03 | coordinates = 135 | coordinates = 47 | coordinates = 5.52 | omitted = JP-29 | type = Unknown | area = Length: 30 m
Height: 8 m | burial = Double-winged [[yokoana-style stone chamber]] (Iwayayama style)
(Contains a hewn [[house-shaped sarcophagus]]) | Excavated Items = | Satellite Tombs = | built = [[7th century]] | Buried Figure = | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = Designated historic site of Nara Prefecture: "Kotani Kofun" | Tangible Cultural Property = | notes = | map = Japan Nara | type = | map_dot_label = Kotani Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kotani Kofun''' (小谷古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Kashihara|ja|橿原市|12=draft}}, [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is designation1 as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == The Kotani Kofun is situated on the southern slope of a ridge extending northeast from Kaibukiyama at the southern edge of the [[Nara Basin]]Historic Site Explanation Board.. The mound is part of a cluster of eight kofun in the area, positioned at the easternmost point of the group. The mound has suffered significant erosion, and no archaeological excavations have been conducted. The mound's type is uncertain but is hypothesized to be either circular or square. It measures approximately 30 meters in length and 8 meters in height. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} with double sidewalls, opening to the south. The stone chamber is constructed with well-cut granite blocks and features a sophisticated design similar to the {{ill|Iwayayama Kofun|ja|岩屋山古墳|12=draft}} in {{ill|Asuka|ja|明日香村|12=draft}}, making it an example of the "Iwayayama-style stone chamber." Within the chamber lies a {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=draft}} carved from [[Hyogo Prefecture]]'s Tatsuyama Stone (stratified hyaloclastite)Tatsuyama Stone has been identified as rhyolitic stratified hyaloclastite (water-cooled pyroclastic rock) based on recent studies. See [https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html Tatsuyama Stone (Hyogo Prefectural Website)] for details.. The grave goods have not been confirmed due to the lack of excavation. The mound dates to the [[7th century]] during the late [[Kofun period]]. It is recognized as one of the representative terminal period kofun in Nara Prefecture, featuring a stone chamber comparable in map_size to those used for royal tombs. While the identity of the interred is unknown, the burial is speculated to belong to an influential aristocratic family, possibly of royal lineage. In the [[Edo period]], it was suggested to be the tomb of [[Empress Saimei]]. The site was designation1 as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site in [[1975]] (Showa 50). Currently, access to the interior of the stone chamber is restricted. Nearby is the Kotani East Kofun (whose stone chamber remains unopened), and it has been proposed that the two kofun form a twin Kofun pairKunihiko Kawakami, *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Fusosha, 2004, 11. 70-72.. == burial == [[File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird's-eye view of the stone chamber.}}]] [[File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Detailed layout of the stone chamber.}}]] [[File:Kotani Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the carved sarcophagus.}}]] The burial facility comprises a double sidewall corridor-style stone chamber, opening to the south. Its area are as follows: * '''Total length:''' A11roximately 11.6 meters * '''Main chamber:''' Length ~5 meters, Width ~2.8 meters, Height ~2.7 meters * '''Entrance corridor:''' Length ~6.45 meters, Width ~1.9 meters, Height ~1.8 meters The chamber is constructed from large, cut granite blocks with meticulous craftsmanship. Both the rear and side walls of the main chamber consist of two layers, and the seams are sealed with plaster. A single large stone serves as the ceiling. The main chamber floor is elevated one step above the corridor. Inside the main chamber lies a house-shaped sarcophagus carved from Tatsuyama Stone[https://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/kotani.html Kotani Kofun] (Kashihara City "Kashihara Visit Navi").. The lid, almost semicylindrical in type, lacks suspension lugs, indicating a newer style within house-shaped sarcophagi. The sarcophagus measures 2.4 meters in length, 1.16 meters in width, and 0.82 meters in height. The chamber shares features with the Iwayayama Kofun, classified as "Iwayayama-style." While the main chambers are similar in size, the corridor of Kotani Kofun is shorter. The stone chamber is also nearly identical in design to the {{ill|Minezuka Kofun|ja|峯塚古墳|12=draft}} in {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市|12=draft}}, suggesting a shift from local clan control of stonework to central administration under the imperial court during the terminal kofun periodKunihiko Kawakami, *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Fusosha, 2004, 11. 66-69.. Minezuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Reference: Bird's-eye view of the Minezuka Kofun stone chamber Kotani Kofun, genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the rear wall) Kotani Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Carved house-shaped sarcophagus inside the main chamber Kotani Kofun, genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Kotani Kofun, sendou-2-2.jpg|Entrance corridor (view toward the entrance) Kotani Kofun, sendou-1-2.jpg|Entrance corridor (view toward the main chamber) Kotani Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Entrance == Historic Site == === Nara Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Kotani Kofun: Designated on March 31, 1975 (Showa 50){{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf Nara Prefecture Cultural Property List]}} (Nara Prefectural Website).. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical site explanation board (installed by the Kashihara City Board of Education). * {{Cite book|year=1981|chapter=Kotani Kofun|title=Nara Prefecture Place names|series=[[Japanese Historical Place name Series]] 30|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Kotani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kotani Kofun}} * [https://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/kotani.html Kotani Kofun] - Kashihara City "Kashihara Visit Navi" {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Kodani Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashihara]] [[Category:Nara Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Kodani Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] }} [[sn:Kodani Kofun]] [[ja:小谷古墳]] [[sn:odani Kofun]]

Nearby is the Kotani East Kofun (whose stone chamber remains unopened), and it has been proposed that the two kofun form a twin Kofun pairKunihiko Kawakami, *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Fusosha, 2004, 11. 70-72.. == burial == [[File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird's-eye view of the stone chamber.}}]] [[File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Detailed layout of the stone chamber.}}]] [[File:Kotani Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the carved sarcophagus.}}]] The burial facility comprises a double sidewall corridor-style stone chamber, opening to the south. Its area are as follows: * '''Total length:''' A11roximately 11.6 meters * '''Main chamber:''' Length ~5 meters, Width ~2.8 meters, Height ~2.7 meters * '''Entrance corridor:''' Length ~6.45 meters, Width ~1.9 meters, Height ~1.8 meters The chamber is constructed from large, cut granite blocks with meticulous craftsmanship. Both the rear and side walls of the main chamber consist of two layers, and the seams are sealed with plaster. A single large stone serves as the ceiling. The main chamber floor is elevated one step above the corridor. Inside the main chamber lies a house-shaped sarcophagus carved from Tatsuyama Stone[https://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/kotani.html Kotani Kofun] (Kashihara City "Kashihara Visit Navi").. The lid, almost semicylindrical in type, lacks suspension lugs, indicating a newer style within house-shaped sarcophagi. The sarcophagus measures 2.4 meters in length, 1.16 meters in width, and 0.82 meters in height. The chamber shares features with the Iwayayama Kofun, classified as "Iwayayama-style." While the main chambers are similar in size, the corridor of Kotani Kofun is shorter. The stone chamber is also nearly identical in design to the {{ill|Minezuka Kofun|ja|峯塚古墳}} in {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市}}, suggesting a shift from local clan control of stonework to central administration under the imperial court during the terminal kofun periodKunihiko Kawakami, *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Fusosha, 2004, 11. 66-69.. Minezuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Reference: Bird's-eye view of the Minezuka Kofun stone chamber Kotani Kofun, genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the rear wall) Kotani Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Carved house-shaped sarcophagus inside the main chamber Kotani Kofun, genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Kotani Kofun, sendou-2-2.jpg|Entrance corridor (view toward the entrance) Kotani Kofun, sendou-1-2.jpg|Entrance corridor (view toward the main chamber) Kotani Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Entrance == Historic Site == === Nara Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Kotani Kofun: Designated on March 31, 1975 (Showa 50){{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf Nara Prefecture Cultural Property List]}} (Nara Prefectural Website).. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical site explanation board (installed by the Kashihara City Board of Education). * {{Cite book|year=1981|chapter=Kotani Kofun|title=Nara Prefecture Place names|series=[[Japanese Historical Place name Series]] 30|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Kotani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kotani Kofun}} * [https://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/kotani.html Kotani Kofun] - Kashihara City "Kashihara Visit Navi" {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Kodani Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashihara]] [[Category:Nara Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Kodani Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] }} [[sn:Kodani Kofun]] [[ja:小谷古墳]] [[sn:odani Kofun]]

revision.comment: 17 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:小谷古墳 up to rev 103773463
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 小谷古墳 |画像 = [[File:Kotani Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
石室開口部 |別名 = |所属 = |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[橿原市]]鳥屋町 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 28|緯度秒 = 29.03 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 47|経度秒 = 5.52 |ISO = JP-29 |形状 = 不明 |規模 = 長さ30m
高さ8m |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]]（岩屋山式）
（内部に刳抜式[[家形石棺]]） |出土品 = |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[7世紀]]代 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 奈良県指定史跡「小谷古墳」 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = |ラベル = 小谷古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''小谷古墳'''（こたにこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[橿原市]]鳥屋町にある[[古墳]]。奈良県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == [[奈良盆地]]南縁、貝吹山から北東に延びる尾根の先端部南斜面に築造された古墳である史跡説明板。。一帯では古墳8基からなる古墳群が分布し、その東端に位置する。これまでに墳丘封土の大部分が流失しているほか、発掘調査は実施されていない。 墳形は明らかでなく、円形または方形と推定され、長さ約30メートル・高さ約8メートルを測る。埋葬施設は両袖式の[[横穴式石室]]で、南方向に開口する。[[花崗岩]]の巨石の切石を用いた整美な大型石室で、[[岩屋山古墳]]（[[明日香村]]）に類似する岩屋山式石室になる。玄室内には兵庫県加古川流域産の成層[[ハイアロクラスタイト]]竜山石は、かつて流紋岩質溶結凝灰岩とされてきたが、近年の調査によって流紋岩質成層ハイアロクラスタイト（水冷破砕岩）と判明している（[https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html 宝殿石（竜山石）]（兵庫県ホームページ）参照）。（竜山石）製の刳抜式[[家形石棺]]を据える。未調査のため副葬品は詳らかでない。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]][[終末期古墳|終末期]]の[[7世紀]]代と推定される。当時としては大王陵クラスの石室が構築された古墳として、奈良県内では代表的な終末期古墳の1つになる。被葬者は明らかでないが、皇族を含めた有力氏族の墓と推定され、[[江戸時代]]には[[斉明天皇]]陵に比定する説も挙げられていた。 古墳域は[[1975年]]（[[昭和]]50年）に奈良県指定史跡に指定されている。現在では石室内部への立ち入りは制限されている。なお、小谷古墳の付近には小谷東古墳（石室は未開口）があり、小谷古墳と小谷東古墳とを2基1対の双墓とする説が挙げられている[[河上邦彦]]『飛鳥発掘物語』扶桑社、2004年、pp. 70-72。。 == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|石室俯瞰図}}]] [[File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石室展開図}}]] [[File:Kotani Kofun, sekkan 3D-C.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石棺立断面図}}]] 埋葬施設としては両袖式[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南方向に開口する。石室の規模は次の通り。 * 石室全長：約11.6メートル * 玄室：長さ約5メートル、幅約2.8メートル、高さ約2.7メートル * 羨道：長さ約6.45メートル、幅1.9メートル、高さ約1.8メートル 石室は[[花崗岩]]の巨石の切石を用いた整美なものである。玄室は奥壁・側壁とも2段積みであり、目地には漆喰が使用される。玄室の天井石は1枚。また玄室の床面は、羨道よりも1段高く構築される。 玄室内には、兵庫県加古川流域産の成層[[ハイアロクラスタイト]]（竜山石）製の刳抜式家形石棺を据える[https://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/kotani.html 小谷古墳]（橿原市「かしはら探訪ナビ」）。。蓋石は蒲鉾形に近く縄掛突起がないという、家形石棺のうちでも新しい型式になる。棺身は長さ2.4メートル・幅1.16メートル・高さ0.82メートルを測る。 石室の形態としては[[岩屋山古墳]]（[[明日香村]]）と同様の特徴を有しており、「岩屋山式石室」と捉えられるが、岩屋山古墳石室とは玄室は同規模であるものの羨道は短縮されている。また、[[峯塚古墳]]（[[天理市]]）石室とはほぼ同一設計になるとして注目される[[河上邦彦]]『飛鳥発掘物語』扶桑社、2004年、pp. 66-69。。前時代（古墳時代後期）には各地域の有力豪族同士で石室が似る例は少ないが、終末期に入ると小谷古墳と峯塚古墳のように地域を超えて同一設計で石室が築造されるようになり、石工集団が豪族管理から朝廷管理に移行した様子が示唆される。 Minezuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|参考：[[峯塚古墳]]石室俯瞰図 Kotani Kofun, genshitsu-1-2.jpg|玄室（奥壁方向） Kotani Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|玄室内の刳抜式家形石棺 Kotani Kofun, genshitsu-2-2.jpg|玄室（開口部方向） Kotani Kofun, sendou-2-2.jpg|羨道（開口部方向） Kotani Kofun, sendou-1-2.jpg|羨道（玄室方向） Kotani Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|開口部 Kotani Kofun, sekkan 3D-B.jpg|石棺俯瞰図 Kotani Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|石棺展開図 == 文化財 == === 奈良県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 小谷古墳 - 1975年（昭和50年）3月31日指定{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf 奈良県指定文化財一覧]}}（奈良県ホームページ）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 史跡説明板（橿原市教育委員会設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=小谷古墳|title=奈良県の地名|series=[[日本歴史地名大系]]30|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|小谷古墳（平凡社）|1981}}}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Kotani Kofun}} * [https://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/kotani.html 小谷古墳] - 橿原市「かしはら探訪ナビ」 {{DEFAULTSORT:こたにこふん}} [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]] [[Category:奈良県指定史跡]]
