{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kotani Kofun | image = [[File:Kotani Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = | part_of = | location = [[Toriya-cho]], [[Kashihara]], [[Nara Prefecture]] | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 28 | coordinates = 29.03 | coordinates = 135 | coordinates = 47 | coordinates = 5.52 | omitted = JP-29 | type = Unknown | area = Length: 30 m
Height: 8 m | burial = Double-winged [[yokoana-style stone chamber]] (Iwayayama style)
(Contains a hewn [[house-shaped sarcophagus]]) | Excavated Items = | Satellite Tombs = | built = [[7th century]] | Buried Figure = | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = Designated historic site of Nara Prefecture: "Kotani Kofun" | Tangible Cultural Property = | notes = | map = Japan Nara | type = | map_dot_label = Kotani Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kotani Kofun''' (小谷古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Kashihara|ja|橿原市}}, [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is designation1 as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == The Kotani Kofun is situated on the southern slope of a ridge extending northeast from Kaibukiyama at the southern edge of the [[Nara Basin]]Historic Site Explanation Board.. The mound is part of a cluster of eight kofun in the area, positioned at the easternmost point of the group. The mound has suffered significant erosion, and no archaeological excavations have been conducted. The mound's type is uncertain but is hypothesized to be either circular or square. It measures approximately 30 meters in length and 8 meters in height. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} with double sidewalls, opening to the south. The stone chamber is constructed with well-cut granite blocks and features a sophisticated design similar to the {{ill|Iwayayama Kofun|ja|岩屋山古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka|ja|明日香村}}, making it an example of the "Iwayayama-style stone chamber." Within the chamber lies a {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} carved from [[Hyogo Prefecture]]'s Tatsuyama Stone (stratified hyaloclastite)Tatsuyama Stone has been identified as rhyolitic stratified hyaloclastite (water-cooled pyroclastic rock) based on recent studies. See [https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html Tatsuyama Stone (Hyogo Prefectural Website)] for details.. The grave goods have not been confirmed due to the lack of excavation. The mound dates to the [[7th century]] during the late [[Kofun period]]. It is recognized as one of the representative terminal period kofun in Nara Prefecture, featuring a stone chamber comparable in map_size to those used for royal tombs. While the identity of the interred is unknown, the burial is speculated to belong to an influential aristocratic family, possibly of royal lineage. In the [[Edo period]], it was suggested to be the tomb of [[Empress Saimei]]. The site was designation1 as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site in [[1975]] (Showa 50). Currently, access to the interior of the stone chamber is restricted. Nearby is the Kotani East Kofun (whose stone chamber remains unopened), and it has been proposed that the two kofun form a twin Kofun pairKunihiko Kawakami, *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Fusosha, 2004, 11. 70-72.. == burial == [[File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird's-eye view of the stone chamber.}}]] [[File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Detailed layout of the stone chamber.}}]] [[File:Kotani Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the carved sarcophagus.}}]] The burial facility comprises a double sidewall corridor-style stone chamber, opening to the south. Its area are as follows: * '''Total length:''' A11roximately 11.6 meters * '''Main chamber:''' Length ~5 meters, Width ~2.8 meters, Height ~2.7 meters * '''Entrance corridor:''' Length ~6.45 meters, Width ~1.9 meters, Height ~1.8 meters The chamber is constructed from large, cut granite blocks with meticulous craftsmanship. Both the rear and side walls of the main chamber consist of two layers, and the seams are sealed with plaster. A single large stone serves as the ceiling. The main chamber floor is elevated one step above the corridor. Inside the main chamber lies a house-shaped sarcophagus carved from Tatsuyama Stone[https://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/kotani.html Kotani Kofun] (Kashihara City "Kashihara Visit Navi").. The lid, almost semicylindrical in type, lacks suspension lugs, indicating a newer style within house-shaped sarcophagi. The sarcophagus measures 2.4 meters in length, 1.16 meters in width, and 0.82 meters in height. The chamber shares features with the Iwayayama Kofun, classified as "Iwayayama-style." While the main chambers are similar in size, the corridor of Kotani Kofun is shorter. The stone chamber is also nearly identical in design to the {{ill|Minezuka Kofun|ja|峯塚古墳}} in {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市}}, suggesting a shift from local clan control of stonework to central administration under the imperial court during the terminal kofun periodKunihiko Kawakami, *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Fusosha, 2004, 11. 66-69.. Minezuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Reference: Bird's-eye view of the Minezuka Kofun stone chamber Kotani Kofun, genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the rear wall) Kotani Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Carved house-shaped sarcophagus inside the main chamber Kotani Kofun, genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Kotani Kofun, sendou-2-2.jpg|Entrance corridor (view toward the entrance) Kotani Kofun, sendou-1-2.jpg|Entrance corridor (view toward the main chamber) Kotani Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Entrance == Historic Site == === Nara Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Kotani Kofun: Designated on March 31, 1975 (Showa 50){{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf Nara Prefecture Cultural Property List]}} (Nara Prefectural Website).. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical site explanation board (installed by the Kashihara City Board of Education). * {{Cite book|year=1981|chapter=Kotani Kofun|title=Nara Prefecture Place names|series=[[Japanese Historical Place name Series]] 30|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Kotani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kotani Kofun}} * [https://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/kotani.html Kotani Kofun] - Kashihara City "Kashihara Visit Navi" {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Kodani Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashihara]] [[Category:Nara Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Kodani Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] }} [[sn:Kodani Kofun]] [[ja:小谷古墳]] [[sn:odani Kofun]] {{translated page|ja|小谷古墳|version=103773463|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Height: 8 m | burial = Double-winged [[yokoana-style stone chamber]] (Iwayayama style)
(Contains a hewn [[house-shaped sarcophagus]]) | Excavated Items = | Satellite Tombs = | built = [[7th century]] | Buried Figure = | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = Designated historic site of Nara Prefecture: "Kotani Kofun" | Tangible Cultural Property = | notes = | map = Japan Nara | type = | map_dot_label = Kotani Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kotani Kofun''' (小谷古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Kashihara|ja|橿原市|qq=draft}}, [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is designation1 as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == The Kotani Kofun is situated on the southern slope of a ridge extending northeast from Kaibukiyama at the southern edge of the [[Nara Basin]]Historic Site Explanation Board.. The mound is part of a cluster of eight kofun in the area, positioned at the easternmost point of the group. The mound has suffered significant erosion, and no archaeological excavations have been conducted. The mound's type is uncertain but is hypothesized to be either circular or square. It measures approximately 30 meters in length and 8 meters in height. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft}} with double sidewalls, opening to the south. The stone chamber is constructed with well-cut granite blocks and features a sophisticated design similar to the {{ill|Iwayayama Kofun|ja|岩屋山古墳|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Asuka|ja|明日香村|qq=draft}}, making it an example of the "Iwayayama-style stone chamber." Within the chamber lies a {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft}} carved from [[Hyogo Prefecture]]'s Tatsuyama Stone (stratified hyaloclastite)Tatsuyama Stone has been identified as rhyolitic stratified hyaloclastite (water-cooled pyroclastic rock) based on recent studies. See [https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html Tatsuyama Stone (Hyogo Prefectural Website)] for details.. The grave goods have not been confirmed due to the lack of excavation. The mound dates to the [[7th century]] during the late [[Kofun period]]. It is recognized as one of the representative terminal period kofun in Nara Prefecture, featuring a stone chamber comparable in map_size to those used for royal tombs. While the identity of the interred is unknown, the burial is speculated to belong to an influential aristocratic family, possibly of royal lineage. In the [[Edo period]], it was suggested to be the tomb of [[Empress Saimei]]. The site was designation1 as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site in [[1975]] (Showa 50). Currently, access to the interior of the stone chamber is restricted. Nearby is the Kotani East Kofun (whose stone chamber remains unopened), and it has been proposed that the two kofun form a twin Kofun pairKunihiko Kawakami, *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Fusosha, 2004, 11. 70-72.. == burial == [[File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird's-eye view of the stone chamber.}}]] [[File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Detailed layout of the stone chamber.}}]] [[File:Kotani Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the carved sarcophagus.}}]] The burial facility comprises a double sidewall corridor-style stone chamber, opening to the south. Its area are as follows: * '''Total length:''' A11roximately 11.6 meters * '''Main chamber:''' Length ~5 meters, Width ~2.8 meters, Height ~2.7 meters * '''Entrance corridor:''' Length ~6.45 meters, Width ~1.9 meters, Height ~1.8 meters The chamber is constructed from large, cut granite blocks with meticulous craftsmanship. Both the rear and side walls of the main chamber consist of two layers, and the seams are sealed with plaster. A single large stone serves as the ceiling. The main chamber floor is elevated one step above the corridor. Inside the main chamber lies a house-shaped sarcophagus carved from Tatsuyama Stone[https://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/kotani.html Kotani Kofun] (Kashihara City "Kashihara Visit Navi").. The lid, almost semicylindrical in type, lacks suspension lugs, indicating a newer style within house-shaped sarcophagi. The sarcophagus measures 2.4 meters in length, 1.16 meters in width, and 0.82 meters in height. The chamber shares features with the Iwayayama Kofun, classified as "Iwayayama-style." While the main chambers are similar in size, the corridor of Kotani Kofun is shorter. The stone chamber is also nearly identical in design to the {{ill|Minezuka Kofun|ja|峯塚古墳|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市|qq=draft}}, suggesting a shift from local clan control of stonework to central administration under the imperial court during the terminal kofun periodKunihiko Kawakami, *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Fusosha, 2004, 11. 66-69.. Minezuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Reference: Bird's-eye view of the Minezuka Kofun stone chamber Kotani Kofun, genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the rear wall) Kotani Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Carved house-shaped sarcophagus inside the main chamber Kotani Kofun, genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Kotani Kofun, sendou-2-2.jpg|Entrance corridor (view toward the entrance) Kotani Kofun, sendou-1-2.jpg|Entrance corridor (view toward the main chamber) Kotani Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Entrance == Historic Site == === Nara Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Kotani Kofun: Designated on March 31, 1975 (Showa 50){{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf Nara Prefecture Cultural Property List]}} (Nara Prefectural Website).. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical site explanation board (installed by the Kashihara City Board of Education). * {{Cite book|year=1981|chapter=Kotani Kofun|title=Nara Prefecture Place names|series=[[Japanese Historical Place name Series]] 30|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Kotani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kotani Kofun}} * [https://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/kotani.html Kotani Kofun] - Kashihara City "Kashihara Visit Navi" {{citation needed|Kodani Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小谷古墳|version=103773463|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashihara]] [[Category:Nara Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Kodani Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] }} [[sn:Kodani Kofun]] [[ja:小谷古墳]] [[sn:odani Kofun]]

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kotani Kofun | image = [[File:Kotani Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = | part_of = | location = [[Toriya-cho]], [[Kashihara]], [[Nara Prefecture]] | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 28 | coordinates = 29.03 | coordinates = 135 | coordinates = 47 | coordinates = 5.52 | omitted = JP-29 | type = Unknown | area = Length: 30 m
Height: 8 m | burial = Double-winged [[yokoana-style stone chamber]] (Iwayayama style)
(Contains a hewn [[house-shaped sarcophagus]]) | Excavated Items = | Satellite Tombs = | built = [[7th century]] | Buried Figure = | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = Designated historic site of Nara Prefecture: "Kotani Kofun" | Tangible Cultural Property = | notes = | map = Japan Nara | type = | map_dot_label = Kotani Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kotani Kofun''' (小谷古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Kashihara|ja|橿原市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashihara|qq=}}, [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is designation1 as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == The Kotani Kofun is situated on the southern slope of a ridge extending northeast from Kaibukiyama at the southern edge of the [[Nara Basin]]Historic Site Explanation Board.. The mound is part of a cluster of eight kofun in the area, positioned at the easternmost point of the group. The mound has suffered significant erosion, and no archaeological excavations have been conducted. The mound's type is uncertain but is hypothesized to be either circular or square. It measures approximately 30 meters in length and 8 meters in height. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with double sidewalls, opening to the south. The stone chamber is constructed with well-cut granite blocks and features a sophisticated design similar to the {{ill|Iwayayama Kofun|ja|岩屋山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwayayama Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Asuka|ja|明日香村|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka|qq=}}, making it an example of the "Iwayayama-style stone chamber." Within the chamber lies a {{ill|house-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} carved from [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]'s Tatsuyama Stone (stratified hyaloclastite)Tatsuyama Stone has been identified as rhyolitic stratified hyaloclastite (water-cooled pyroclastic rock) based on recent studies. See [https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html Tatsuyama Stone (Hyogo Prefectural Website)] for details.. The grave goods have not been confirmed due to the lack of excavation. The mound dates to the [[7th century]] during the late [[Kofun period]]. It is recognized as one of the representative terminal period kofun in Nara Prefecture, featuring a stone chamber comparable in map_size to those used for royal tombs. While the identity of the interred is unknown, the burial is speculated to belong to an influential aristocratic family, possibly of royal lineage. In the [[Edo period]], it was suggested to be the tomb of [[Empress Saimei]]. The site was designation1 as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site in [[1975]] (Showa 50). Currently, access to the interior of the stone chamber is restricted. Nearby is the Kotani East Kofun (whose stone chamber remains unopened), and it has been proposed that the two kofun form a twin Kofun pairKunihiko Kawakami, *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Fusosha, 2004, 11. 70-72.. == burial == [[File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird's-eye view of the stone chamber.}}]] [[File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Detailed layout of the stone chamber.}}]] [[File:Kotani Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the carved sarcophagus.}}]] The burial facility comprises a double sidewall corridor-style stone chamber, opening to the south. Its area are as follows: * '''Total length:''' A11roximately 11.6 meters * '''Main chamber:''' Length ~5 meters, Width ~2.8 meters, Height ~2.7 meters * '''Entrance corridor:''' Length ~6.45 meters, Width ~1.9 meters, Height ~1.8 meters The chamber is constructed from large, cut granite blocks with meticulous craftsmanship. Both the rear and side walls of the main chamber consist of two layers, and the seams are sealed with plaster. A single large stone serves as the ceiling. The main chamber floor is elevated one step above the corridor. Inside the main chamber lies a house-shaped sarcophagus carved from Tatsuyama Stone[https://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/kotani.html Kotani Kofun] (Kashihara City "Kashihara Visit Navi").. The lid, almost semicylindrical in type, lacks suspension lugs, indicating a newer style within house-shaped sarcophagi. The sarcophagus measures 2.4 meters in length, 1.16 meters in width, and 0.82 meters in height. The chamber shares features with the Iwayayama Kofun, classified as "Iwayayama-style." While the main chambers are similar in size, the corridor of Kotani Kofun is shorter. The stone chamber is also nearly identical in design to the {{ill|Minezuka Kofun|ja|峯塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minezuka Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenri|qq=}}, suggesting a shift from local clan control of stonework to central administration under the imperial court during the terminal kofun periodKunihiko Kawakami, *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Fusosha, 2004, 11. 66-69.. Minezuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Reference: Bird's-eye view of the Minezuka Kofun stone chamber Kotani Kofun, genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the rear wall) Kotani Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Carved house-shaped sarcophagus inside the main chamber Kotani Kofun, genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Kotani Kofun, sendou-2-2.jpg|Entrance corridor (view toward the entrance) Kotani Kofun, sendou-1-2.jpg|Entrance corridor (view toward the main chamber) Kotani Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Entrance == Historic Site == === Nara Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Kotani Kofun: Designated on March 31, 1975 (Showa 50){{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf Nara Prefecture Cultural Property List]}} (Nara Prefectural Website).. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical site explanation board (installed by the Kashihara City Board of Education). * {{Cite book|year=1981|chapter=Kotani Kofun|title=Nara Prefecture Place names|series=[[Japanese Historical Place name Series]] 30|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Kotani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kotani Kofun}} * [https://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/kotani.html Kotani Kofun] - Kashihara City "Kashihara Visit Navi" {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小谷古墳|version=103773463|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashihara]] [[Category:Nara Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Kodani Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] }} [[sn:Kodani Kofun]] [[ja:小谷古墳]] [[sn:odani Kofun]]

It is recognized as one of the representative terminal period kofun in Nara Prefecture, featuring a stone chamber comparable in map_size to those used for royal tombs. While the identity of the interred is unknown, the burial is speculated to belong to an influential aristocratic family, possibly of royal lineage. In the [[Edo period]], it was suggested to be the tomb of [[Empress Saimei]]. The site was designation1 as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site in [[1975]] (Showa 50). Currently, access to the interior of the stone chamber is restricted. Nearby is the Kotani East Kofun (whose stone chamber remains unopened), and it has been proposed that the two kofun form a twin Kofun pairKunihiko Kawakami, *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Fusosha, 2004, 11. 70-72.. == burial == [[File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird's-eye view of the stone chamber.}}]] [[File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Detailed layout of the stone chamber.}}]] [[File:Kotani Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the carved sarcophagus.}}]] The burial facility comprises a double sidewall corridor-style stone chamber, opening to the south. Its area are as follows: * '''Total length:''' A11roximately 11.6 meters * '''Main chamber:''' Length ~5 meters, Width ~2.8 meters, Height ~2.7 meters * '''Entrance corridor:''' Length ~6.45 meters, Width ~1.9 meters, Height ~1.8 meters The chamber is constructed from large, cut granite blocks with meticulous craftsmanship. Both the rear and side walls of the main chamber consist of two layers, and the seams are sealed with plaster. A single large stone serves as the ceiling. The main chamber floor is elevated one step above the corridor. Inside the main chamber lies a house-shaped sarcophagus carved from Tatsuyama Stone[https://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/kotani.html Kotani Kofun] (Kashihara City "Kashihara Visit Navi").. The lid, almost semicylindrical in type, lacks suspension lugs, indicating a newer style within house-shaped sarcophagi. The sarcophagus measures 2.4 meters in length, 1.16 meters in width, and 0.82 meters in height. The chamber shares features with the Iwayayama Kofun, classified as "Iwayayama-style." While the main chambers are similar in size, the corridor of Kotani Kofun is shorter. The stone chamber is also nearly identical in design to the {{ill|Minezuka Kofun|ja|峯塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minezuka Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenri|qq=}}, suggesting a shift from local clan control of stonework to central administration under the imperial court during the terminal kofun periodKunihiko Kawakami, *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Fusosha, 2004, 11. 66-69.. Minezuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Reference: Bird's-eye view of the Minezuka Kofun stone chamber Kotani Kofun, genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the rear wall) Kotani Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Carved house-shaped sarcophagus inside the main chamber Kotani Kofun, genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Kotani Kofun, sendou-2-2.jpg|Entrance corridor (view toward the entrance) Kotani Kofun, sendou-1-2.jpg|Entrance corridor (view toward the main chamber) Kotani Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Entrance == Historic Site == === Nara Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Kotani Kofun: Designated on March 31, 1975 (Showa 50){{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf Nara Prefecture Cultural Property List]}} (Nara Prefectural Website).. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical site explanation board (installed by the Kashihara City Board of Education). * {{Cite book|year=1981|chapter=Kotani Kofun|title=Nara Prefecture Place names|series=[[Japanese Historical Place name Series]] 30|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Kotani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kotani Kofun}} * [https://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/kotani.html Kotani Kofun] - Kashihara City "Kashihara Visit Navi" {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小谷古墳|version=103773463|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashihara]] [[Category:Nara Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Kodani Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] }} [[sn:Kodani Kofun]] [[ja:小谷古墳]] [[sn:odani Kofun]]

Height: 8 m | burial = Double-winged [[yokoana-style stone chamber]] (Iwayayama style)
(Contains a hewn [[House-shaped Sarcophagus|House-shaped sarcophagus]]) | Excavated Items = | Satellite Tombs = | built = [[7th century]] | Buried Figure = | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = Designated historic site of Nara Prefecture: "Kotani Kofun" | Tangible Cultural Property = | notes = | map = Japan Nara | type = | map_dot_label = Kotani Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kotani Kofun''' (小谷古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Kashihara|ja|橿原市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashihara|qq=}}, [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is designation1 as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == The Kotani Kofun is situated on the southern slope of a ridge extending northeast from Kaibukiyama at the southern edge of the [[Nara Basin]]Historic Site Explanation Board.. The mound is part of a cluster of eight kofun in the area, positioned at the easternmost point of the group. The mound has suffered significant erosion, and no archaeological excavations have been conducted. The mound's type is uncertain but is hypothesized to be either circular or square. It measures approximately 30 meters in length and 8 meters in height. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with double sidewalls, opening to the south. The stone chamber is constructed with well-cut granite blocks and features a sophisticated design similar to the {{ill|Iwayayama Kofun|ja|岩屋山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwayayama Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Asuka|ja|明日香村|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka|qq=}}, making it an example of the "Iwayayama-style stone chamber." Within the chamber lies a {{ill|House-shaped Sarcophagus|lt=House-shaped Sarcophagus|lt=House-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|lt=House-shaped sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} carved from [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]'s Tatsuyama Stone (stratified hyaloclastite)Tatsuyama Stone has been identified as rhyolitic stratified hyaloclastite (water-cooled pyroclastic rock) based on recent studies. See [https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html Tatsuyama Stone (Hyōgo Prefectural Website)] for details.. The grave goods have not been confirmed due to the lack of excavation. The mound dates to the [[7th century]] during the late [[Kofun period]]. It is recognized as one of the representative terminal period kofun in Nara Prefecture, featuring a stone chamber comparable in map_size to those used for royal tombs. While the identity of the interred is unknown, the burial is speculated to belong to an influential aristocratic family, possibly of royal lineage. In the [[Edo period]], it was suggested to be the tomb of [[Empress Saimei]]. The site was designation1 as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site in [[1975]] (Showa 50). Currently, access to the interior of the stone chamber is restricted. Nearby is the Kotani East Kofun (whose stone chamber remains unopened), and it has been proposed that the two kofun form a twin Kofun pairKunihiko Kawakami, *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Fusosha, 2004, 11. 70-72.. == burial == [[File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird's-eye view of the stone chamber.}}]] [[File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Detailed layout of the stone chamber.}}]] [[File:Kotani Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the carved sarcophagus.}}]] The burial facility comprises a double sidewall corridor-style stone chamber, opening to the south. The lid, almost semicylindrical in type, lacks suspension lugs, indicating a newer style within house-shaped sarcophagi. The sarcophagus measures 2.4 meters in length, 1.16 meters in width, and 0.82 meters in height. The chamber shares features with the Iwayayama Kofun, classified as "Iwayayama-style." While the main chambers are similar in size, the corridor of Kotani Kofun is shorter. (Contains a hewn [[House-shaped Sarcophagus|House-shaped sarcophagus]]) | Excavated Items = | Satellite Tombs = | built = [[7th century]] | Buried Figure = | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = Designated historic site of Nara Prefecture: "Kotani Kofun" | Tangible Cultural Property = | notes = | map = Japan Nara | type = | map_dot_label = Kotani Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kotani Kofun''' (小谷古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is designation1 as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == The Kotani Kofun is situated on the southern slope of a ridge extending northeast from Kaibukiyama at the southern edge of the [[Nara Basin]]Historic Site Explanation Board.. The mound is part of a cluster of eight kofun in the area, positioned at the easternmost point of the group. The mound has suffered significant erosion, and no archaeological excavations have been conducted. The mound's type is uncertain but is hypothesized to be either circular or square. It measures approximately 30 meters in length and 8 meters in height. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with double sidewalls, opening to the south. The stone chamber is constructed with well-cut granite blocks and features a sophisticated design similar to the [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]] in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka]], making it an example of the "Iwayayama-style stone chamber." Within the chamber lies a {{ill|House-shaped Sarcophagus|lt=House-shaped Sarcophagus|lt=House-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|lt=House-shaped sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} carved from [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]'s Tatsuyama Stone (stratified hyaloclastite)Tatsuyama Stone has been identified as rhyolitic stratified hyaloclastite (water-cooled pyroclastic rock) based on recent studies. See [https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html Tatsuyama Stone (Hyōgo Prefectural Website)] for details.. The grave goods have not been confirmed due to the lack of excavation. The mound dates to the [[7th century]] during the late [[Kofun period]]. It is recognized as one of the representative terminal period kofun in Nara Prefecture, featuring a stone chamber comparable in map_size to those used for royal tombs. While the identity of the interred is unknown, the burial is speculated to belong to an influential aristocratic family, possibly of royal lineage. In the [[Edo period]], it was suggested to be the tomb of [[Empress Saimei]]. The site was designation1 as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site in [[1975]] (Showa 50). Currently, access to the interior of the stone chamber is restricted. Nearby is the Kotani East Kofun (whose stone chamber remains unopened), and it has been proposed that the two kofun form a twin Kofun pairKunihiko Kawakami, *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Fusosha, 2004, 11. 70-72.. == burial == [[File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird's-eye view of the stone chamber.}}]] [[File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Detailed layout of the stone chamber.}}]] [[File:Kotani Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the carved sarcophagus.}}]] The burial facility comprises a double sidewall corridor-style stone chamber, opening to the south. Its area are as follows: * '''Total length:''' A11roximately 11.6 meters * '''Main chamber:''' Length ~5 meters, Width ~2.8 meters, Height ~2.7 meters * '''Entrance corridor:''' Length ~6.45 meters, Width ~1.9 meters, Height ~1.8 meters The chamber is constructed from large, cut granite blocks with meticulous craftsmanship. Both the rear and side walls of the main chamber consist of two layers, and the seams are sealed with plaster. A single large stone serves as the ceiling. The main chamber floor is elevated one step above the corridor. Inside the main chamber lies a house-shaped sarcophagus carved from Tatsuyama Stone[https://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/kotani.html Kotani Kofun] (Kashihara City "Kashihara Visit Navi").. The lid, almost semicylindrical in type, lacks suspension lugs, indicating a newer style within house-shaped sarcophagi. The sarcophagus measures 2.4 meters in length, 1.16 meters in width, and 0.82 meters in height. The chamber shares features with the Iwayayama Kofun, classified as "Iwayayama-style." While the main chambers are similar in size, the corridor of Kotani Kofun is shorter. The stone chamber is also nearly identical in design to the {{ill|Minezuka Kofun|ja|峯塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minezuka Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]], suggesting a shift from local clan control of stonework to central administration under the imperial court during the terminal kofun periodKunihiko Kawakami, *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Fusosha, 2004, 11. 66-69.. Minezuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Reference: Bird's-eye view of the Minezuka Kofun stone chamber Kotani Kofun, genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the rear wall) Kotani Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Carved house-shaped sarcophagus inside the main chamber Kotani Kofun, genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Kotani Kofun, sendou-2-2.jpg|Entrance corridor (view toward the entrance) Kotani Kofun, sendou-1-2.jpg|Entrance corridor (view toward the main chamber) Kotani Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Entrance == Historic Site == === Nara Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Kotani Kofun: Designated on March 31, 1975 (Showa 50){{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf Nara Prefecture Cultural Property List]}} (Nara Prefectural Website).. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical site explanation board (installed by the Kashihara City Board of Education). * {{Cite book|year=1981|chapter=Kotani Kofun|title=Nara Prefecture Place names|series=[[Japanese Historical Place name Series]] 30|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Kotani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kotani Kofun}} * [https://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/kotani.html Kotani Kofun] - Kashihara City "Kashihara Visit Navi" {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小谷古墳|version=103773463|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashihara]] [[Category:Nara Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] }} [[sn:Kodani Kofun]] [[ja:小谷古墳]] [[sn:odani Kofun]] [[Category:奈良県の古墳]]

Minezuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Reference: Bird's-eye view of the Minezuka Kofun stone chamber Kotani Kofun, genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the rear wall) Kotani Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Carved house-shaped sarcophagus inside the main chamber Kotani Kofun, genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Kotani Kofun, sendou-2-2.jpg|Entrance corridor (view toward the entrance) Kotani Kofun, sendou-1-2.jpg|Entrance corridor (view toward the main chamber) Kotani Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Entrance == Historic Site == === Nara Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Kotani Kofun: Designated on March 31, 1975 (Showa 50){{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf Nara Prefecture Cultural Property List]}} (Nara Prefectural Website).. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical site explanation board (installed by the Kashihara City Board of Education). * {{Cite book|year=1981|chapter=Kotani Kofun|title=Nara Prefecture Place names|series=[[Japanese Historical Place name Series]] 30|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Kotani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kotani Kofun}} * [https://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/kotani.html Kotani Kofun] - Kashihara City "Kashihara Visit Navi" {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小谷古墳|version=103773463|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashihara]] [[Category:Nara Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] }} [[sn:Kodani Kofun]] [[ja:小谷古墳]] [[sn:odani Kofun]] [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:奈良県指定史跡]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]]

revision.comment: Bot: replace redirect category → [[Category:奈良県の古墳]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kotani Kofun | image = [[File:Kotani Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = | part_of = | location = [[Toriya-cho]], [[Kashihara]], [[Nara Prefecture]] | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 28 | coordinates = 29.03 | coordinates = 135 | coordinates = 47 | coordinates = 5.52 | omitted = JP-29 | type = Unknown | area = Length: 30 m
Height: 8 m | burial = Double-winged [[yokoana-style stone chamber]] (Iwayayama style)
(Contains a hewn [[House-shaped Sarcophagus|House-shaped sarcophagus]]) | Excavated Items = | Satellite Tombs = | built = [[7th century]] | Buried Figure = | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = Designated historic site of Nara Prefecture: "Kotani Kofun" | Tangible Cultural Property = | notes = | map = Japan Nara | type = | map_dot_label = Kotani Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kotani Kofun''' (小谷古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is designation1 as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == The Kotani Kofun is situated on the southern slope of a ridge extending northeast from Kaibukiyama at the southern edge of the [[Nara Basin]]Historic Site Explanation Board.. The mound is part of a cluster of eight kofun in the area, positioned at the easternmost point of the group. The mound has suffered significant erosion, and no archaeological excavations have been conducted. The mound's type is uncertain but is hypothesized to be either circular or square. It measures approximately 30 meters in length and 8 meters in height. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with double sidewalls, opening to the south. The stone chamber is constructed with well-cut granite blocks and features a sophisticated design similar to the [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]] in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka]], making it an example of the "Iwayayama-style stone chamber." Within the chamber lies a {{ill|House-shaped Sarcophagus|lt=House-shaped Sarcophagus|lt=House-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|lt=House-shaped sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} carved from [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]'s Tatsuyama Stone (stratified hyaloclastite)Tatsuyama Stone has been identified as rhyolitic stratified hyaloclastite (water-cooled pyroclastic rock) based on recent studies. See [https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html Tatsuyama Stone (Hyōgo Prefectural Website)] for details.. The grave goods have not been confirmed due to the lack of excavation. The mound dates to the [[7th century]] during the late [[Kofun period]]. It is recognized as one of the representative terminal period kofun in Nara Prefecture, featuring a stone chamber comparable in map_size to those used for royal tombs. While the identity of the interred is unknown, the burial is speculated to belong to an influential aristocratic family, possibly of royal lineage. In the [[Edo period]], it was suggested to be the tomb of [[Empress Saimei]]. The site was designation1 as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site in [[1975]] (Showa 50). Currently, access to the interior of the stone chamber is restricted. Nearby is the Kotani East Kofun (whose stone chamber remains unopened), and it has been proposed that the two kofun form a twin Kofun pairKunihiko Kawakami, *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Fusosha, 2004, 11. 70-72.. == burial == [[File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird's-eye view of the stone chamber.}}]] [[File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Detailed layout of the stone chamber.}}]] [[File:Kotani Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the carved sarcophagus.}}]] The burial facility comprises a double sidewall corridor-style stone chamber, opening to the south. Its area are as follows: * '''Total length:''' A11roximately 11.6 meters * '''Main chamber:''' Length ~5 meters, Width ~2.8 meters, Height ~2.7 meters * '''Entrance corridor:''' Length ~6.45 meters, Width ~1.9 meters, Height ~1.8 meters The chamber is constructed from large, cut granite blocks with meticulous craftsmanship. Both the rear and side walls of the main chamber consist of two layers, and the seams are sealed with plaster. A single large stone serves as the ceiling. The main chamber floor is elevated one step above the corridor. Inside the main chamber lies a house-shaped sarcophagus carved from Tatsuyama Stone[https://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/kotani.html Kotani Kofun] (Kashihara City "Kashihara Visit Navi").. The lid, almost semicylindrical in type, lacks suspension lugs, indicating a newer style within house-shaped sarcophagi. The sarcophagus measures 2.4 meters in length, 1.16 meters in width, and 0.82 meters in height. The chamber shares features with the Iwayayama Kofun, classified as "Iwayayama-style." While the main chambers are similar in size, the corridor of Kotani Kofun is shorter. The stone chamber is also nearly identical in design to the {{ill|Minezuka Kofun|ja|峯塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minezuka Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]], suggesting a shift from local clan control of stonework to central administration under the imperial court during the terminal kofun periodKunihiko Kawakami, *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Fusosha, 2004, 11. 66-69.. Minezuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Reference: Bird's-eye view of the Minezuka Kofun stone chamber Kotani Kofun, genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the rear wall) Kotani Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Carved house-shaped sarcophagus inside the main chamber Kotani Kofun, genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Kotani Kofun, sendou-2-2.jpg|Entrance corridor (view toward the entrance) Kotani Kofun, sendou-1-2.jpg|Entrance corridor (view toward the main chamber) Kotani Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Entrance == Historic Site == === Nara Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Kotani Kofun: Designated on March 31, 1975 (Showa 50){{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf Nara Prefecture Cultural Property List]}} (Nara Prefectural Website).. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical site explanation board (installed by the Kashihara City Board of Education). * {{Cite book|year=1981|chapter=Kotani Kofun|title=Nara Prefecture Place names|series=[[Japanese Historical Place name Series]] 30|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Kotani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kotani Kofun}} * [https://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/kotani.html Kotani Kofun] - Kashihara City "Kashihara Visit Navi" {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小谷古墳|version=103773463|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:History of Kashihara]] [[Category:Nara Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] }} [[sn:Kodani Kofun]] [[ja:小谷古墳]] [[sn:odani Kofun]] [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:奈良県指定史跡]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]]

The mound dates to the [[7th century]] during the late [[Kofun period]]. It is recognized as one of the representative terminal period kofun in Nara Prefecture, featuring a stone chamber comparable in map_size to those used for royal tombs. While the identity of the interred is unknown, the burial is speculated to belong to an influential aristocratic family, possibly of royal lineage. In the [[Edo period]], it was suggested to be the tomb of [[Empress Saimei]]. The site was designation1 as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site in [[1975]] (Showa 50). Currently, access to the interior of the stone chamber is restricted. Nearby is the Kotani East Kofun (whose stone chamber remains unopened), and it has been proposed that the two kofun form a twin Kofun pairKunihiko Kawakami, *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Fusosha, 2004, 11. 70-72.. == burial == [[File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird's-eye view of the stone chamber.}}]] [[File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Detailed layout of the stone chamber.}}]] [[File:Kotani Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the carved sarcophagus.}}]] The burial facility comprises a double sidewall corridor-style stone chamber, opening to the south. Its area are as follows: * '''Total length:''' A11roximately 11.6 meters * '''Main chamber:''' Length ~5 meters, Width ~2.8 meters, Height ~2.7 meters * '''Entrance corridor:''' Length ~6.45 meters, Width ~1.9 meters, Height ~1.8 meters The chamber is constructed from large, cut granite blocks with meticulous craftsmanship. Both the rear and side walls of the main chamber consist of two layers, and the seams are sealed with plaster. A single large stone serves as the ceiling. The main chamber floor is elevated one step above the corridor. Inside the main chamber lies a house-shaped sarcophagus carved from Tatsuyama Stone[https://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/kotani.html Kotani Kofun] (Kashihara City "Kashihara Visit Navi").. The lid, almost semicylindrical in type, lacks suspension lugs, indicating a newer style within house-shaped sarcophagi. The sarcophagus measures 2.4 meters in length, 1.16 meters in width, and 0.82 meters in height. The chamber shares features with the Iwayayama Kofun, classified as "Iwayayama-style." While the main chambers are similar in size, the corridor of Kotani Kofun is shorter. The stone chamber is also nearly identical in design to the {{ill|Minezuka Kofun|ja|峯塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minezuka Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]], suggesting a shift from local clan control of stonework to central administration under the imperial court during the terminal kofun periodKunihiko Kawakami, *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Fusosha, 2004, 11. 66-69.. Minezuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Reference: Bird's-eye view of the Minezuka Kofun stone chamber Kotani Kofun, genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the rear wall) Kotani Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Carved house-shaped sarcophagus inside the main chamber Kotani Kofun, genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Kotani Kofun, sendou-2-2.jpg|Entrance corridor (view toward the entrance) Kotani Kofun, sendou-1-2.jpg|Entrance corridor (view toward the main chamber) Kotani Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Entrance == Historic Site == === Nara Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Kotani Kofun: Designated on March 31, 1975 (Showa 50){{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf Nara Prefecture Cultural Property List]}} (Nara Prefectural Website).. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical site explanation board (installed by the Kashihara City Board of Education). * {{Cite book|year=1981|chapter=Kotani Kofun|title=Nara Prefecture Place names|series=[[Japanese Historical Place name Series]] 30|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Kotani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kotani Kofun}} * [https://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/kotani.html Kotani Kofun] - Kashihara City "Kashihara Visit Navi" {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小谷古墳|version=103773463|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashihara]] [[Category:Nara Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] }} [[sn:Kodani Kofun]] [[ja:小谷古墳]] [[sn:odani Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]]

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kotani Kofun | image = [[File:Kotani Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = | part_of = | location = [[Toriya-cho]], [[Kashihara]], [[Nara Prefecture]] | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 28 | coordinates = 29.03 | coordinates = 135 | coordinates = 47 | coordinates = 5.52 | omitted = JP-29 | type = Unknown | area = Length: 30 m
Height: 8 m | burial = Double-winged [[yokoana-style stone chamber]] (Iwayayama style)
(Contains a hewn [[House-shaped Sarcophagus|House-shaped sarcophagus]]) | Excavated Items = | Satellite Tombs = | built = [[7th century]] | Buried Figure = | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = Designated historic site of Nara Prefecture: "Kotani Kofun" | Tangible Cultural Property = | notes = | map = Japan Nara | type = | map_dot_label = Kotani Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kotani Kofun''' (小谷古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is designation1 as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == The Kotani Kofun is situated on the southern slope of a ridge extending northeast from Kaibukiyama at the southern edge of the [[Nara Basin]]Historic Site Explanation Board.. The mound is part of a cluster of eight kofun in the area, positioned at the easternmost point of the group. The mound has suffered significant erosion, and no archaeological excavations have been conducted. The mound's type is uncertain but is hypothesized to be either circular or square. It measures approximately 30 meters in length and 8 meters in height. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with double sidewalls, opening to the south. The stone chamber is constructed with well-cut granite blocks and features a sophisticated design similar to the [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]] in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka]], making it an example of the "Iwayayama-style stone chamber." Within the chamber lies a {{ill|House-shaped Sarcophagus|lt=House-shaped Sarcophagus|lt=House-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|lt=House-shaped sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} carved from [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]'s Tatsuyama Stone (stratified hyaloclastite)Tatsuyama Stone has been identified as rhyolitic stratified hyaloclastite (water-cooled pyroclastic rock) based on recent studies. See [https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html Tatsuyama Stone (Hyōgo Prefectural Website)] for details.. The grave goods have not been confirmed due to the lack of excavation. The mound dates to the [[7th century]] during the late [[Kofun period]]. It is recognized as one of the representative terminal period kofun in Nara Prefecture, featuring a stone chamber comparable in map_size to those used for royal tombs. While the identity of the interred is unknown, the burial is speculated to belong to an influential aristocratic family, possibly of royal lineage. In the [[Edo period]], it was suggested to be the tomb of [[Empress Saimei]]. The site was designation1 as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site in [[1975]] (Showa 50). Currently, access to the interior of the stone chamber is restricted. Nearby is the Kotani East Kofun (whose stone chamber remains unopened), and it has been proposed that the two kofun form a twin Kofun pairKunihiko Kawakami, *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Fusosha, 2004, 11. 70-72.. == burial == [[File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird's-eye view of the stone chamber.}}]] [[File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Detailed layout of the stone chamber.}}]] [[File:Kotani Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the carved sarcophagus.}}]] The burial facility comprises a double sidewall corridor-style stone chamber, opening to the south. Its area are as follows: * '''Total length:''' A11roximately 11.6 meters * '''Main chamber:''' Length ~5 meters, Width ~2.8 meters, Height ~2.7 meters * '''Entrance corridor:''' Length ~6.45 meters, Width ~1.9 meters, Height ~1.8 meters The chamber is constructed from large, cut granite blocks with meticulous craftsmanship. Both the rear and side walls of the main chamber consist of two layers, and the seams are sealed with plaster. A single large stone serves as the ceiling. The main chamber floor is elevated one step above the corridor. Inside the main chamber lies a house-shaped sarcophagus carved from Tatsuyama Stone[https://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/kotani.html Kotani Kofun] (Kashihara City "Kashihara Visit Navi").. The lid, almost semicylindrical in type, lacks suspension lugs, indicating a newer style within house-shaped sarcophagi. The sarcophagus measures 2.4 meters in length, 1.16 meters in width, and 0.82 meters in height. The chamber shares features with the Iwayayama Kofun, classified as "Iwayayama-style." While the main chambers are similar in size, the corridor of Kotani Kofun is shorter. The stone chamber is also nearly identical in design to the {{ill|Minezuka Kofun|ja|峯塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minezuka Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]], suggesting a shift from local clan control of stonework to central administration under the imperial court during the terminal kofun periodKunihiko Kawakami, *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Fusosha, 2004, 11. 66-69.. It is designation1 as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == The Kotani Kofun is situated on the southern slope of a ridge extending northeast from Kaibukiyama at the southern edge of the [[Nara Basin]]Historic Site Explanation Board.. The mound is part of a cluster of eight kofun in the area, positioned at the easternmost point of the group. The mound has suffered significant erosion, and no archaeological excavations have been conducted. The mound's type is uncertain but is hypothesized to be either circular or square. It measures approximately 30 meters in length and 8 meters in height. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with double sidewalls, opening to the south. The stone chamber is constructed with well-cut granite blocks and features a sophisticated design similar to the [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]] in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka]], making it an example of the "Iwayayama-style stone chamber." Within the chamber lies a {{ill|House-shaped Sarcophagus|lt=House-shaped Sarcophagus|lt=House-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|lt=House-shaped sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} carved from [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]'s Tatsuyama Stone (stratified hyaloclastite)Tatsuyama Stone has been identified as rhyolitic stratified hyaloclastite (water-cooled pyroclastic rock) based on recent studies. See [https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html Tatsuyama Stone (Hyōgo Prefectural Website)] for details.. The grave goods have not been confirmed due to the lack of excavation. The mound dates to the [[7th century]] during the late [[Kofun period]]. It is recognized as one of the representative terminal period kofun in Nara Prefecture, featuring a stone chamber comparable in map_size to those used for royal tombs. While the identity of the interred is unknown, the burial is speculated to belong to an influential aristocratic family, possibly of royal lineage. In the [[Edo period]], it was suggested to be the tomb of [[Empress Saimei]]. The site was designation1 as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site in [[1975]] (Showa 50). Currently, access to the interior of the stone chamber is restricted. Nearby is the Kotani East Kofun (whose stone chamber remains unopened), and it has been proposed that the two kofun form a twin Kofun pairKunihiko Kawakami, *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Fusosha, 2004, 11. 70-72.. == burial == [[File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird's-eye view of the stone chamber.}}]] [[File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Detailed layout of the stone chamber.}}]] [[File:Kotani Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the carved sarcophagus.}}]] The burial facility comprises a double sidewall corridor-style stone chamber, opening to the south. Its area are as follows: * '''Total length:''' A11roximately 11.6 meters * '''Main chamber:''' Length ~5 meters, Width ~2.8 meters, Height ~2.7 meters * '''Entrance corridor:''' Length ~6.45 meters, Width ~1.9 meters, Height ~1.8 meters The chamber is constructed from large, cut granite blocks with meticulous craftsmanship. Both the rear and side walls of the main chamber consist of two layers, and the seams are sealed with plaster. A single large stone serves as the ceiling. The main chamber floor is elevated one step above the corridor. Inside the main chamber lies a house-shaped sarcophagus carved from Tatsuyama Stone[https://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/kotani.html Kotani Kofun] (Kashihara City "Kashihara Visit Navi").. The lid, almost semicylindrical in type, lacks suspension lugs, indicating a newer style within house-shaped sarcophagi. The sarcophagus measures 2.4 meters in length, 1.16 meters in width, and 0.82 meters in height. The chamber shares features with the Iwayayama Kofun, classified as "Iwayayama-style." While the main chambers are similar in size, the corridor of Kotani Kofun is shorter. The stone chamber is also nearly identical in design to the {{ill|Minezuka Kofun|ja|峯塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minezuka Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]], suggesting a shift from local clan control of stonework to central administration under the imperial court during the terminal kofun periodKunihiko Kawakami, *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Fusosha, 2004, 11. 66-69.. Minezuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Reference: Bird's-eye view of the Minezuka Kofun stone chamber Kotani Kofun, genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the rear wall) Kotani Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Carved house-shaped sarcophagus inside the main chamber Kotani Kofun, genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Kotani Kofun, sendou-2-2.jpg|Entrance corridor (view toward the entrance) Kotani Kofun, sendou-1-2.jpg|Entrance corridor (view toward the main chamber) Kotani Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Entrance == Historic Site == === Nara Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Kotani Kofun: Designated on March 31, 1975 (Showa 50){{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf Nara Prefecture Cultural Property List]}} (Nara Prefectural Website).. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical site explanation board (installed by the Kashihara City Board of Education). * {{Cite book|year=1981|chapter=Kotani Kofun|title=Nara Prefecture Place names|series=[[Japanese Historical Place name Series]] 30|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Kotani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kotani Kofun}} * [https://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/kotani.html Kotani Kofun] - Kashihara City "Kashihara Visit Navi" {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小谷古墳|version=103773463|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashihara]] [[Category:Nara Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] }} [[sn:Kodani Kofun]] [[ja:小谷古墳]] [[sn:odani Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashihara, Nara]] {{Commons category|Kotani Kofun}}
