Minezuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Reference: Bird's-eye view of the Minezuka Kofun stone chamber Kotani Kofun, genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the rear wall) Kotani Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Carved house-shaped sarcophagus inside the main chamber Kotani Kofun, genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Kotani Kofun, sendou-2-2.jpg|Entrance corridor (view toward the entrance) Kotani Kofun, sendou-1-2.jpg|Entrance corridor (view toward the main chamber) Kotani Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Entrance == Historic Site == === Nara Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Kotani Kofun: Designated on March 31, 1975 (Showa 50){{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf Nara Prefecture Cultural Property List]}} (Nara Prefectural Website).. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical site explanation board (installed by the Kashihara City Board of Education). * {{Cite book|year=1981|chapter=Kotani Kofun|title=Nara Prefecture Place names|series=[[Japanese Historical Place name Series]] 30|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Kotani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kotani Kofun}} * [https://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/kotani.html Kotani Kofun] - Kashihara City "Kashihara Visit Navi" {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小谷古墳|version=103773463|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:小谷古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kotani Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q82961585}} [[Category:Pages linked to by wikidata]] [[Category:Matching pagename on wikidata]]

[[commons:Category:Kotani Kofun]][[ja:小谷古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kotani Kofun | image = [[File:Kotani Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = | part_of = | location = {{ill|Kashihara|qid=Q496319}} | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 28 | coordinates = 29.03 | coordinates = 135 | coordinates = 47 | coordinates = 5.52 | omitted = JP-29 | type = Unknown | area = Length: 30 m
Height: 8 m | burial = Double-winged [[yokoana-style stone chamber]] (Iwayayama style)
(Contains a hewn [[House-shaped Sarcophagus|House-shaped sarcophagus]]) | Excavated Items = | Satellite Tombs = | built = [[:en:7th century|7th century]] | Buried Figure = | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = Designated historic site of Nara Prefecture: "Kotani Kofun" | Tangible Cultural Property = | notes = | map = Japan Nara | type = | map_dot_label = Kotani Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kotani Kofun''' (小谷古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is designation1 as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == The Kotani Kofun is situated on the southern slope of a ridge extending northeast from Kaibukiyama at the southern edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]Historic Site Explanation Board.. The mound is part of a cluster of eight kofun in the area, positioned at the easternmost point of the group. The mound has suffered significant erosion, and no archaeological excavations have been conducted. The mound's type is uncertain but is hypothesized to be either circular or square. It measures approximately 30 meters in length and 8 meters in height. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with double sidewalls, opening to the south. The stone chamber is constructed with well-cut granite blocks and features a sophisticated design similar to the [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]] in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka]], making it an example of the "Iwayayama-style stone chamber." Within the chamber lies a {{ill|House-shaped Sarcophagus|lt=House-shaped Sarcophagus|lt=House-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|lt=House-shaped sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} carved from [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]'s Tatsuyama Stone (stratified hyaloclastite)Tatsuyama Stone has been identified as rhyolitic stratified hyaloclastite (water-cooled pyroclastic rock) based on recent studies. See [https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html Tatsuyama Stone (Hyōgo Prefectural Website)] for details.. The grave goods have not been confirmed due to the lack of excavation. The mound dates to the [[:en:7th century|7th century]] during the late [[Kofun period]]. It is recognized as one of the representative terminal period kofun in Nara Prefecture, featuring a stone chamber comparable in map_size to those used for royal tombs. While the identity of the interred is unknown, the burial is speculated to belong to an influential aristocratic family, possibly of royal lineage. In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was suggested to be the tomb of [[:en:Empress Saimei|Empress Saimei]]. The site was designationed as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site in [[:en:1975|1975]] (Showa 50). Currently, access to the interior of the stone chamber is restricted. Nearby is the Kotani East Kofun (whose stone chamber remains unopened), and it has been proposed that the two kofun form a twin Kofun pairKunihiko Kawakami, *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Fusosha, 2004, 11. 70-72.. == burial == [[File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird's-eye view of the stone chamber.}}]] [[File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Detailed layout of the stone chamber.}}]] [[File:Kotani Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the carved sarcophagus.}}]] The burial facility comprises a double sidewall corridor-style stone chamber, opening to the south. Its area are as follows: * '''Total length:''' Approximately11.6 meters * '''Main chamber:''' Length ~5 meters, Width ~2.8 meters, Height ~2.7 meters * '''Entrance corridor:''' Length ~6.45 meters, Width ~1.9 meters, Height ~1.8 meters The chamber is constructed from large, cut granite blocks with meticulous craftsmanship. Both the rear and side walls of the main chamber consist of two layers, and the seams are sealed with plaster. A single large stone serves as the ceiling. The main chamber floor is elevated one step above the corridor. Inside the main chamber lies a house-shaped sarcophagus carved from Tatsuyama Stone[https://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/kotani.html Kotani Kofun] (Kashihara City "Kashihara Visit Navi").. The lid, almost semicylindrical in type, lacks suspension lugs, indicating a newer style within house-shaped sarcophagi. The sarcophagus measures 2.4 meters in length, 1.16 meters in width, and 0.82 meters in height. The chamber shares features with the Iwayayama Kofun, classified as "Iwayayama-style." While the main chambers are similar in size, the corridor of Kotani Kofun is shorter. The stone chamber is also nearly identical in design to the {{ill|Minezuka Kofun|ja|峯塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minezuka Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]], suggesting a shift from local clan control of stonework to central administration under the imperial court during the terminal kofun periodKunihiko Kawakami, *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Fusosha, 2004, 11. 66-69.. Minezuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Reference: Bird's-eye view of the Minezuka Kofun stone chamber Kotani Kofun, genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the rear wall) Kotani Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Carved house-shaped sarcophagus inside the main chamber Kotani Kofun, genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Kotani Kofun, sendou-2-2.jpg|Entrance corridor (view toward the entrance) Kotani Kofun, sendou-1-2.jpg|Entrance corridor (view toward the main chamber) Kotani Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Entrance == Historic Site == === Nara Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Kotani Kofun: Designated on March 31, 1975 (Showa 50){{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf Nara Prefecture Cultural Property List]}} (Nara Prefectural Website).. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical site explanation board (installed by the Kashihara City Board of Education). * {{Cite book|year=1981|chapter=Kotani Kofun|title=Nara Prefecture Place names|series=[[Japanese Historical Place name Series]] 30|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Kotani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kotani Kofun}} * [https://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/kotani.html Kotani Kofun] - Kashihara City "Kashihara Visit Navi" {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小谷古墳|version=103773463|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:小谷古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kotani Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q82961585}} [[Category:Pages linked to by wikidata]] [[Category:Matching pagename on wikidata]]

Minezuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Reference: Bird's-eye view of the Minezuka Kofun stone chamber Kotani Kofun, genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the rear wall) Kotani Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Carved house-shaped sarcophagus inside the main chamber Kotani Kofun, genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Kotani Kofun, sendou-2-2.jpg|Entrance corridor (view toward the entrance) Kotani Kofun, sendou-1-2.jpg|Entrance corridor (view toward the main chamber) Kotani Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Entrance == Historic Site == === Nara Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Kotani Kofun: Designated on March 31, 1975 (Showa 50){{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf Nara Prefecture Cultural Property List]}} (Nara Prefectural Website).. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical site explanation board (installed by the Kashihara City Board of Education). * {{Cite book|year=1981|chapter=Kotani Kofun|title=Nara Prefecture Place names|series=[[Japanese Historical Place name Series]] 30|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Kotani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kotani Kofun}} * [https://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/kotani.html Kotani Kofun] - Kashihara City "Kashihara Visit Navi" {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小谷古墳|version=103773463|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:小谷古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kotani Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q82961585}} [[Category:Pages linked to by wikidata]] [[Category:Matching pagename on wikidata]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Kotani Kofun]][[ja:小谷古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kotani Kofun | image = [[File:Kotani Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = | part_of = | location = {{ill|Kashihara|WD=Q496319}} | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 28 | coordinates = 29.03 | coordinates = 135 | coordinates = 47 | coordinates = 5.52 | omitted = JP-29 | type = Unknown | area = Length: 30 m
Height: 8 m | burial = Double-winged [[yokoana-style stone chamber]] (Iwayayama style)
(Contains a hewn [[House-shaped Sarcophagus|House-shaped sarcophagus]]) | Excavated Items = | Satellite Tombs = | built = [[:en:7th century|7th century]] | Buried Figure = | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = Designated historic site of Nara Prefecture: "Kotani Kofun" | Tangible Cultural Property = | notes = | map = Japan Nara | type = | map_dot_label = Kotani Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kotani Kofun''' (小谷古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is designation1 as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == The Kotani Kofun is situated on the southern slope of a ridge extending northeast from Kaibukiyama at the southern edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]Historic Site Explanation Board.. The mound is part of a cluster of eight kofun in the area, positioned at the easternmost point of the group. The mound has suffered significant erosion, and no archaeological excavations have been conducted. The mound's type is uncertain but is hypothesized to be either circular or square. It measures approximately 30 meters in length and 8 meters in height. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with double sidewalls, opening to the south. The stone chamber is constructed with well-cut granite blocks and features a sophisticated design similar to the [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]] in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka]], making it an example of the "Iwayayama-style stone chamber." Within the chamber lies a {{ill|House-shaped Sarcophagus|lt=House-shaped Sarcophagus|lt=House-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|lt=House-shaped sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} carved from [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]'s Tatsuyama Stone (stratified hyaloclastite)Tatsuyama Stone has been identified as rhyolitic stratified hyaloclastite (water-cooled pyroclastic rock) based on recent studies. See [https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html Tatsuyama Stone (Hyōgo Prefectural Website)] for details.. The grave goods have not been confirmed due to the lack of excavation. The mound dates to the [[:en:7th century|7th century]] during the late [[Kofun period]]. It is recognized as one of the representative terminal period kofun in Nara Prefecture, featuring a stone chamber comparable in map_size to those used for royal tombs. While the identity of the interred is unknown, the burial is speculated to belong to an influential aristocratic family, possibly of royal lineage. In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was suggested to be the tomb of [[:en:Empress Saimei|Empress Saimei]]. The site was designationed as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site in [[:en:1975|1975]] (Showa 50). Currently, access to the interior of the stone chamber is restricted. Nearby is the Kotani East Kofun (whose stone chamber remains unopened), and it has been proposed that the two kofun form a twin Kofun pairKunihiko Kawakami, *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Fusosha, 2004, 11. 70-72.. == burial == [[File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird's-eye view of the stone chamber.}}]] [[File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Detailed layout of the stone chamber.}}]] [[File:Kotani Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the carved sarcophagus.}}]] The burial facility comprises a double sidewall corridor-style stone chamber, opening to the south. Its area are as follows: * '''Total length:''' Approximately11.6 meters * '''Main chamber:''' Length ~5 meters, Width ~2.8 meters, Height ~2.7 meters * '''Entrance corridor:''' Length ~6.45 meters, Width ~1.9 meters, Height ~1.8 meters The chamber is constructed from large, cut granite blocks with meticulous craftsmanship. Both the rear and side walls of the main chamber consist of two layers, and the seams are sealed with plaster. A single large stone serves as the ceiling. The main chamber floor is elevated one step above the corridor. Inside the main chamber lies a house-shaped sarcophagus carved from Tatsuyama Stone[https://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/kotani.html Kotani Kofun] (Kashihara City "Kashihara Visit Navi").. The lid, almost semicylindrical in type, lacks suspension lugs, indicating a newer style within house-shaped sarcophagi. The sarcophagus measures 2.4 meters in length, 1.16 meters in width, and 0.82 meters in height. The chamber shares features with the Iwayayama Kofun, classified as "Iwayayama-style." While the main chambers are similar in size, the corridor of Kotani Kofun is shorter. The stone chamber is also nearly identical in design to the {{ill|Minezuka Kofun|ja|峯塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minezuka Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]], suggesting a shift from local clan control of stonework to central administration under the imperial court during the terminal kofun periodKunihiko Kawakami, *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Fusosha, 2004, 11. 66-69.. Minezuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Reference: Bird's-eye view of the Minezuka Kofun stone chamber Kotani Kofun, genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the rear wall) Kotani Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Carved house-shaped sarcophagus inside the main chamber Kotani Kofun, genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Kotani Kofun, sendou-2-2.jpg|Entrance corridor (view toward the entrance) Kotani Kofun, sendou-1-2.jpg|Entrance corridor (view toward the main chamber) Kotani Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Entrance == Historic Site == === Nara Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Kotani Kofun: Designated on March 31, 1975 (Showa 50){{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf Nara Prefecture Cultural Property List]}} (Nara Prefectural Website).. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical site explanation board (installed by the Kashihara City Board of Education). * {{Cite book|year=1981|chapter=Kotani Kofun|title=Nara Prefecture Place names|series=[[Japanese Historical Place name Series]] 30|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Kotani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kotani Kofun}} * [https://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/kotani.html Kotani Kofun] - Kashihara City "Kashihara Visit Navi" {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小谷古墳|version=103773463|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:小谷古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kotani Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q82961585}} [[Category:Pages linked to by wikidata]] [[Category:Matching pagename on wikidata]]

Minezuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Reference: Bird's-eye view of the Minezuka Kofun stone chamber Kotani Kofun, genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the rear wall) Kotani Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Carved house-shaped sarcophagus inside the main chamber Kotani Kofun, genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Kotani Kofun, sendou-2-2.jpg|Entrance corridor (view toward the entrance) Kotani Kofun, sendou-1-2.jpg|Entrance corridor (view toward the main chamber) Kotani Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Entrance == Historic Site == === Nara Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Kotani Kofun: Designated on March 31, 1975 (Showa 50){{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf Nara Prefecture Cultural Property List]}} (Nara Prefectural Website).. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical site explanation board (installed by the Kashihara City Board of Education). * {{Cite book|year=1981|chapter=Kotani Kofun|title=Nara Prefecture Place names|series=[[Japanese Historical Place name Series]] 30|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Kotani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kotani Kofun}} * [https://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/kotani.html Kotani Kofun] - Kashihara City "Kashihara Visit Navi" {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小谷古墳|version=103773463|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:小谷古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kotani Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q82961585}}

revision.comment: Bot: Add Wikidata no short description - based on Wikidata English short description
revision.text:
[[sn:commons:Category:Kotani Kofun]][[ja:小谷古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kotani Kofun | image = [[File:Kotani Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = | part_of = | location = {{ill|Kashihara|WD=Q496319}} | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 28 | coordinates = 29.03 | coordinates = 135 | coordinates = 47 | coordinates = 5.52 | omitted = JP-29 | type = Unknown | area = Length: 30 m
Height: 8 m | burial = Double-winged [[yokoana-style stone chamber]] (Iwayayama style)
(Contains a hewn [[House-shaped Sarcophagus|House-shaped sarcophagus]]) | Excavated Items = | Satellite Tombs = | built = [[:en:7th century|7th century]] | Buried Figure = | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = Designated historic site of Nara Prefecture: "Kotani Kofun" | Tangible Cultural Property = | notes = | map = Japan Nara | type = | map_dot_label = Kotani Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kotani Kofun''' (小谷古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is designation1 as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == The Kotani Kofun is situated on the southern slope of a ridge extending northeast from Kaibukiyama at the southern edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]Historic Site Explanation Board.. The mound is part of a cluster of eight kofun in the area, positioned at the easternmost point of the group. The mound has suffered significant erosion, and no archaeological excavations have been conducted. The mound's type is uncertain but is hypothesized to be either circular or square. It measures approximately 30 meters in length and 8 meters in height. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with double sidewalls, opening to the south. The stone chamber is constructed with well-cut granite blocks and features a sophisticated design similar to the [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]] in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka]], making it an example of the "Iwayayama-style stone chamber." Within the chamber lies a {{ill|House-shaped Sarcophagus|lt=House-shaped Sarcophagus|lt=House-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|lt=House-shaped sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} carved from [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]'s Tatsuyama Stone (stratified hyaloclastite)Tatsuyama Stone has been identified as rhyolitic stratified hyaloclastite (water-cooled pyroclastic rock) based on recent studies. See [https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html Tatsuyama Stone (Hyōgo Prefectural Website)] for details.. The grave goods have not been confirmed due to the lack of excavation. The mound dates to the [[:en:7th century|7th century]] during the late [[Kofun period]]. It is recognized as one of the representative terminal period kofun in Nara Prefecture, featuring a stone chamber comparable in map_size to those used for royal tombs. While the identity of the interred is unknown, the burial is speculated to belong to an influential aristocratic family, possibly of royal lineage. In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was suggested to be the tomb of [[:en:Empress Saimei|Empress Saimei]]. The site was designationed as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site in [[:en:1975|1975]] (Showa 50). Currently, access to the interior of the stone chamber is restricted. Nearby is the Kotani East Kofun (whose stone chamber remains unopened), and it has been proposed that the two kofun form a twin Kofun pairKunihiko Kawakami, *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Fusosha, 2004, 11. 70-72.. == burial == [[File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird's-eye view of the stone chamber.}}]] [[File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Detailed layout of the stone chamber.}}]] [[File:Kotani Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the carved sarcophagus.}}]] The burial facility comprises a double sidewall corridor-style stone chamber, opening to the south. Its area are as follows: * '''Total length:''' Approximately11.6 meters * '''Main chamber:''' Length ~5 meters, Width ~2.8 meters, Height ~2.7 meters * '''Entrance corridor:''' Length ~6.45 meters, Width ~1.9 meters, Height ~1.8 meters The chamber is constructed from large, cut granite blocks with meticulous craftsmanship. Both the rear and side walls of the main chamber consist of two layers, and the seams are sealed with plaster. A single large stone serves as the ceiling. The main chamber floor is elevated one step above the corridor. Inside the main chamber lies a house-shaped sarcophagus carved from Tatsuyama Stone[https://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/kotani.html Kotani Kofun] (Kashihara City "Kashihara Visit Navi").. The lid, almost semicylindrical in type, lacks suspension lugs, indicating a newer style within house-shaped sarcophagi. The sarcophagus measures 2.4 meters in length, 1.16 meters in width, and 0.82 meters in height. The chamber shares features with the Iwayayama Kofun, classified as "Iwayayama-style." While the main chambers are similar in size, the corridor of Kotani Kofun is shorter. The stone chamber is also nearly identical in design to the {{ill|Minezuka Kofun|ja|峯塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minezuka Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]], suggesting a shift from local clan control of stonework to central administration under the imperial court during the terminal kofun periodKunihiko Kawakami, *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Fusosha, 2004, 11. 66-69.. Minezuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Reference: Bird's-eye view of the Minezuka Kofun stone chamber Kotani Kofun, genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the rear wall) Kotani Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Carved house-shaped sarcophagus inside the main chamber Kotani Kofun, genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Kotani Kofun, sendou-2-2.jpg|Entrance corridor (view toward the entrance) Kotani Kofun, sendou-1-2.jpg|Entrance corridor (view toward the main chamber) Kotani Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Entrance == Historic Site == === Nara Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Kotani Kofun: Designated on March 31, 1975 (Showa 50){{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf Nara Prefecture Cultural Property List]}} (Nara Prefectural Website).. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical site explanation board (installed by the Kashihara City Board of Education). * {{Cite book|year=1981|chapter=Kotani Kofun|title=Nara Prefecture Place names|series=[[Japanese Historical Place name Series]] 30|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Kotani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kotani Kofun}} * [https://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/kotani.html Kotani Kofun] - Kashihara City "Kashihara Visit Navi" {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小谷古墳|version=103773463|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:小谷古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kotani Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q82961585}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

Minezuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Reference: Bird's-eye view of the Minezuka Kofun stone chamber Kotani Kofun, genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the rear wall) Kotani Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Carved house-shaped sarcophagus inside the main chamber Kotani Kofun, genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Kotani Kofun, sendou-2-2.jpg|Entrance corridor (view toward the entrance) Kotani Kofun, sendou-1-2.jpg|Entrance corridor (view toward the main chamber) Kotani Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Entrance == Historic Site == === Nara Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Kotani Kofun: Designated on March 31, 1975 (Showa 50){{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf Nara Prefecture Cultural Property List]}} (Nara Prefectural Website).. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical site explanation board (installed by the Kashihara City Board of Education). * {{Cite book|year=1981|chapter=Kotani Kofun|title=Nara Prefecture Place names|series=[[Japanese Historical Place name Series]] 30|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Kotani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kotani Kofun}} * [https://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/kotani.html Kotani Kofun] - Kashihara City "Kashihara Visit Navi" {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小谷古墳|version=103773463|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:小谷古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kotani Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q82961585}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Kotani Kofun]][[ja:小谷古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kotani Kofun | image = [[File:Kotani Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = | part_of = | location = {{ill|Kashihara|WD=Q496319}} | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 28 | coordinates = 29.03 | coordinates = 135 | coordinates = 47 | coordinates = 5.52 | omitted = JP-29 | type = Unknown | area = Length: 30 m
Height: 8 m | burial = Double-winged [[yokoana-style stone chamber]] (Iwayayama style)
(Contains a hewn [[House-shaped Sarcophagus|House-shaped sarcophagus]]) | Excavated Items = | Satellite Tombs = | built = [[:en:7th century|7th century]] | Buried Figure = | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = Designated historic site of Nara Prefecture: "Kotani Kofun" | Tangible Cultural Property = | notes = | map = Japan Nara | type = | map_dot_label = Kotani Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kotani Kofun''' (小谷古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is designation1 as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == The Kotani Kofun is situated on the southern slope of a ridge extending northeast from Kaibukiyama at the southern edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]Historic Site Explanation Board.. The mound is part of a cluster of eight kofun in the area, positioned at the easternmost point of the group. The mound has suffered significant erosion, and no archaeological excavations have been conducted. The mound's type is uncertain but is hypothesized to be either circular or square. It measures approximately 30 meters in length and 8 meters in height. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with double sidewalls, opening to the south. The stone chamber is constructed with well-cut granite blocks and features a sophisticated design similar to the [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]] in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka]], making it an example of the "Iwayayama-style stone chamber." Within the chamber lies a {{ill|House-shaped Sarcophagus|lt=House-shaped Sarcophagus|lt=House-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|lt=House-shaped sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} carved from [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]'s Tatsuyama Stone (stratified hyaloclastite)Tatsuyama Stone has been identified as rhyolitic stratified hyaloclastite (water-cooled pyroclastic rock) based on recent studies. See [https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html Tatsuyama Stone (Hyōgo Prefectural Website)] for details.. The grave goods have not been confirmed due to the lack of excavation. The mound dates to the [[:en:7th century|7th century]] during the late [[Kofun period]]. It is recognized as one of the representative terminal period kofun in Nara Prefecture, featuring a stone chamber comparable in map_size to those used for royal tombs. While the identity of the interred is unknown, the burial is speculated to belong to an influential aristocratic family, possibly of royal lineage. In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was suggested to be the tomb of [[:en:Empress Saimei|Empress Saimei]]. The site was designationed as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site in [[:en:1975|1975]] (Showa 50). Currently, access to the interior of the stone chamber is restricted. Nearby is the Kotani East Kofun (whose stone chamber remains unopened), and it has been proposed that the two kofun form a twin Kofun pairKunihiko Kawakami, *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Fusosha, 2004, 11. 70-72.. == burial == [[File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird's-eye view of the stone chamber.}}]] [[File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Detailed layout of the stone chamber.}}]] [[File:Kotani Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the carved sarcophagus.}}]] The burial facility comprises a double sidewall corridor-style stone chamber, opening to the south. Its area are as follows: * '''Total length:''' Approximately11.6 meters * '''Main chamber:''' Length ~5 meters, Width ~2.8 meters, Height ~2.7 meters * '''Entrance corridor:''' Length ~6.45 meters, Width ~1.9 meters, Height ~1.8 meters The chamber is constructed from large, cut granite blocks with meticulous craftsmanship. Both the rear and side walls of the main chamber consist of two layers, and the seams are sealed with plaster. A single large stone serves as the ceiling. The main chamber floor is elevated one step above the corridor. Inside the main chamber lies a house-shaped sarcophagus carved from Tatsuyama Stone[https://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/kotani.html Kotani Kofun] (Kashihara City "Kashihara Visit Navi").. The lid, almost semicylindrical in type, lacks suspension lugs, indicating a newer style within house-shaped sarcophagi. The sarcophagus measures 2.4 meters in length, 1.16 meters in width, and 0.82 meters in height. The chamber shares features with the Iwayayama Kofun, classified as "Iwayayama-style." While the main chambers are similar in size, the corridor of Kotani Kofun is shorter. The stone chamber is also nearly identical in design to the {{ill|Minezuka Kofun|ja|峯塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minezuka Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]], suggesting a shift from local clan control of stonework to central administration under the imperial court during the terminal kofun periodKunihiko Kawakami, *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Fusosha, 2004, 11. 66-69.. Minezuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Reference: Bird's-eye view of the Minezuka Kofun stone chamber Kotani Kofun, genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the rear wall) Kotani Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Carved house-shaped sarcophagus inside the main chamber Kotani Kofun, genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Kotani Kofun, sendou-2-2.jpg|Entrance corridor (view toward the entrance) Kotani Kofun, sendou-1-2.jpg|Entrance corridor (view toward the main chamber) Kotani Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Entrance == Historic Site == === Nara Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Kotani Kofun: Designated on March 31, 1975 (Showa 50){{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf Nara Prefecture Cultural Property List]}} (Nara Prefectural Website).. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical site explanation board (installed by the Kashihara City Board of Education). * {{Cite book|year=1981|chapter=Kotani Kofun|title=Nara Prefecture Place names|series=[[Japanese Historical Place name Series]] 30|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Kotani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kotani Kofun}} * [https://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/kotani.html Kotani Kofun] - Kashihara City "Kashihara Visit Navi" {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小谷古墳|version=103773463|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:小谷古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kotani Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q82961585}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Minezuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Reference: Bird's-eye view of the Minezuka Kofun stone chamber Kotani Kofun, genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the rear wall) Kotani Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Carved house-shaped sarcophagus inside the main chamber Kotani Kofun, genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Kotani Kofun, sendou-2-2.jpg|Entrance corridor (view toward the entrance) Kotani Kofun, sendou-1-2.jpg|Entrance corridor (view toward the main chamber) Kotani Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Entrance == Historic Site == === Nara Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Kotani Kofun: Designated on March 31, 1975 (Showa 50){{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf Nara Prefecture Cultural Property List]}} (Nara Prefectural Website).. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical site explanation board (installed by the Kashihara City Board of Education). * {{Cite book|year=1981|chapter=Kotani Kofun|title=Nara Prefecture Place names|series=[[Japanese Historical Place name Series]] 30|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Kotani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kotani Kofun}} * [https://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/kotani.html Kotani Kofun] - revision.text:
[[sn:commons:Category:Kotani Kofun]][[ja:小谷古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kotani Kofun | image = [[File:Kotani Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = | part_of = | location = {{ill|Kashihara|WD=Q496319}} | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 28 | coordinates = 29.03 | coordinates = 135 | coordinates = 47 | coordinates = 5.52 | omitted = JP-29 | type = Unknown | area = Length: 30 m
Height: 8 m | burial = Double-winged [[yokoana-style stone chamber]] (Iwayayama style)
(Contains a hewn [[House-shaped Sarcophagus|House-shaped sarcophagus]]) | Excavated Items = | Satellite Tombs = | built = [[:en:7th century|7th century]] | Buried Figure = | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = Designated historic site of Nara Prefecture: "Kotani Kofun" | Tangible Cultural Property = | notes = | map = Japan Nara | type = | map_dot_label = Kotani Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kotani Kofun''' (小谷古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is designation1 as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == The Kotani Kofun is situated on the southern slope of a ridge extending northeast from Kaibukiyama at the southern edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]Historic Site Explanation Board.. The mound is part of a cluster of eight kofun in the area, positioned at the easternmost point of the group. The mound has suffered significant erosion, and no archaeological excavations have been conducted. The mound's type is uncertain but is hypothesized to be either circular or square. It measures approximately 30 meters in length and 8 meters in height. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with double sidewalls, opening to the south. The stone chamber is constructed with well-cut granite blocks and features a sophisticated design similar to the [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]] in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka]], making it an example of the "Iwayayama-style stone chamber." Within the chamber lies a {{ill|House-shaped Sarcophagus|lt=House-shaped Sarcophagus|lt=House-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|lt=House-shaped sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} carved from [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]'s Tatsuyama Stone (stratified hyaloclastite)Tatsuyama Stone has been identified as rhyolitic stratified hyaloclastite (water-cooled pyroclastic rock) based on recent studies. See [https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html Tatsuyama Stone (Hyōgo Prefectural Website)] for details.. The grave goods have not been confirmed due to the lack of excavation. The mound dates to the [[:en:7th century|7th century]] during the late [[Kofun period]]. It is recognized as one of the representative terminal period kofun in Nara Prefecture, featuring a stone chamber comparable in map_size to those used for royal tombs. While the identity of the interred is unknown, the burial is speculated to belong to an influential aristocratic family, possibly of royal lineage. In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was suggested to be the tomb of [[:en:Empress Saimei|Empress Saimei]]. The site was designationed as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site in [[:en:1975|1975]] (Showa 50). Currently, access to the interior of the stone chamber is restricted. Nearby is the Kotani East Kofun (whose stone chamber remains unopened), and it has been proposed that the two kofun form a twin Kofun pairKunihiko Kawakami, *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Fusosha, 2004, 11. 70-72.. == burial == [[File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird's-eye view of the stone chamber.}}]] [[File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Detailed layout of the stone chamber.}}]] [[File:Kotani Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the carved sarcophagus.}}]] The burial facility comprises a double sidewall corridor-style stone chamber, opening to the south. Its area are as follows: * '''Total length:''' Approximately11.6 meters * '''Main chamber:''' Length ~5 meters, Width ~2.8 meters, Height ~2.7 meters * '''Entrance corridor:''' Length ~6.45 meters, Width ~1.9 meters, Height ~1.8 meters The chamber is constructed from large, cut granite blocks with meticulous craftsmanship. Both the rear and side walls of the main chamber consist of two layers, and the seams are sealed with plaster. A single large stone serves as the ceiling. The main chamber floor is elevated one step above the corridor. Inside the main chamber lies a house-shaped sarcophagus carved from Tatsuyama Stone[https://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/kotani.html Kotani Kofun] (Kashihara City "Kashihara Visit Navi").. The lid, almost semicylindrical in type, lacks suspension lugs, indicating a newer style within house-shaped sarcophagi. The sarcophagus measures 2.4 meters in length, 1.16 meters in width, and 0.82 meters in height. The chamber shares features with the Iwayayama Kofun, classified as "Iwayayama-style." While the main chambers are similar in size, the corridor of Kotani Kofun is shorter. The stone chamber is also nearly identical in design to the {{ill|Minezuka Kofun|ja|峯塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minezuka Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]], suggesting a shift from local clan control of stonework to central administration under the imperial court during the terminal kofun periodKunihiko Kawakami, *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Fusosha, 2004, 11. 66-69.. Minezuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Reference: Bird's-eye view of the Minezuka Kofun stone chamber Kotani Kofun, genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the rear wall) Kotani Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Carved house-shaped sarcophagus inside the main chamber Kotani Kofun, genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Kotani Kofun, sendou-2-2.jpg|Entrance corridor (view toward the entrance) Kotani Kofun, sendou-1-2.jpg|Entrance corridor (view toward the main chamber) Kotani Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Entrance == Historic Site == === Nara Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Kotani Kofun: Designated on March 31, 1975 (Showa 50){{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf Nara Prefecture Cultural Property List]}} (Nara Prefectural Website).. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical site explanation board (installed by the Kashihara City Board of Education). * {{Cite book|year=1981|chapter=Kotani Kofun|title=Nara Prefecture Place names|series=[[Japanese Historical Place name Series]] 30|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Kotani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kotani Kofun}} * [https://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/kotani.html Kotani Kofun] - Kashihara City "Kashihara Visit Navi" {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小谷古墳|version=103773463|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:小谷古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kotani Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q82961585}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

Minezuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Reference: Bird's-eye view of the Minezuka Kofun stone chamber Kotani Kofun, genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the rear wall) Kotani Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Carved house-shaped sarcophagus inside the main chamber Kotani Kofun, genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Kotani Kofun, sendou-2-2.jpg|Entrance corridor (view toward the entrance) Kotani Kofun, sendou-1-2.jpg|Entrance corridor (view toward the main chamber) Kotani Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Entrance == Historic Site == === Nara Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Kotani Kofun: Designated on March 31, 1975 (Showa 50){{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf Nara Prefecture Cultural Property List]}} (Nara Prefectural Website).. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical site explanation board (installed by the Kashihara City Board of Education). * {{Cite book|year=1981|chapter=Kotani Kofun|title=Nara Prefecture Place names|series=[[Japanese Historical Place name Series]] 30|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Kotani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kotani Kofun}} * [https://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/kotani.html Kotani Kofun] - Kashihara City "Kashihara Visit Navi" {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小谷古墳|version=103773463|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:小谷古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kotani Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q82961585}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Kotani Kofun]][[ja:小谷古墳]] [[commons:Category:Kotani Kofun]][[ja:小谷古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kotani Kofun]][[ja:小谷古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kotani Kofun | image = [[File:Kotani Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = | part_of = | location = {{ill|Kashihara|WD=Q496319}} | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 28 | coordinates = 29.03 | coordinates = 135 | coordinates = 47 | coordinates = 5.52 | omitted = JP-29 | type = Unknown | area = Length: 30 m
Height: 8 m | burial = Double-winged [[yokoana-style stone chamber]] (Iwayayama style)
(Contains a hewn [[House-shaped Sarcophagus|House-shaped sarcophagus]]) | Excavated Items = | Satellite Tombs = | built = [[:en:7th century|7th century]] | Buried Figure = | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = Designated historic site of Nara Prefecture: "Kotani Kofun" | Tangible Cultural Property = | notes = | map = Japan Nara | type = | map_dot_label = Kotani Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kotani Kofun''' (小谷古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is designation1 as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == The Kotani Kofun is situated on the southern slope of a ridge extending northeast from Kaibukiyama at the southern edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]Historic Site Explanation Board.. The mound is part of a cluster of eight kofun in the area, positioned at the easternmost point of the group. The mound has suffered significant erosion, and no archaeological excavations have been conducted. The mound's type is uncertain but is hypothesized to be either circular or square. It measures approximately 30 meters in length and 8 meters in height. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with double sidewalls, opening to the south. The stone chamber is constructed with well-cut granite blocks and features a sophisticated design similar to the [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]] in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka]], making it an example of the "Iwayayama-style stone chamber." Within the chamber lies a {{ill|House-shaped Sarcophagus|lt=House-shaped Sarcophagus|lt=House-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|lt=House-shaped sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} carved from [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]'s Tatsuyama Stone (stratified hyaloclastite)Tatsuyama Stone has been identified as rhyolitic stratified hyaloclastite (water-cooled pyroclastic rock) based on recent studies. See [https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html Tatsuyama Stone (Hyōgo Prefectural Website)] for details.. The grave goods have not been confirmed due to the lack of excavation. The mound dates to the [[:en:7th century|7th century]] during the late [[Kofun period]]. It is recognized as one of the representative terminal period kofun in Nara Prefecture, featuring a stone chamber comparable in map_size to those used for royal tombs. While the identity of the interred is unknown, the burial is speculated to belong to an influential aristocratic family, possibly of royal lineage. In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was suggested to be the tomb of [[:en:Empress Saimei|Empress Saimei]]. The site was designationed as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site in [[:en:1975|1975]] (Showa 50). Currently, access to the interior of the stone chamber is restricted. Nearby is the Kotani East Kofun (whose stone chamber remains unopened), and it has been proposed that the two kofun form a twin Kofun pairKunihiko Kawakami, *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Fusosha, 2004, 11. 70-72.. == burial == [[File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird's-eye view of the stone chamber.}}]] [[File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Detailed layout of the stone chamber.}}]] [[File:Kotani Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the carved sarcophagus.}}]] The burial facility comprises a double sidewall corridor-style stone chamber, opening to the south. Its area are as follows: * '''Total length:''' Approximately11.6 meters * '''Main chamber:''' Length ~5 meters, Width ~2.8 meters, Height ~2.7 meters * '''Entrance corridor:''' Length ~6.45 meters, Width ~1.9 meters, Height ~1.8 meters The chamber is constructed from large, cut granite blocks with meticulous craftsmanship. Both the rear and side walls of the main chamber consist of two layers, and the seams are sealed with plaster. A single large stone serves as the ceiling. The main chamber floor is elevated one step above the corridor. Inside the main chamber lies a house-shaped sarcophagus carved from Tatsuyama Stone[https://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/kotani.html Kotani Kofun] (Kashihara City "Kashihara Visit Navi").. The lid, almost semicylindrical in type, lacks suspension lugs, indicating a newer style within house-shaped sarcophagi. The sarcophagus measures 2.4 meters in length, 1.16 meters in width, and 0.82 meters in height. The chamber shares features with the Iwayayama Kofun, classified as "Iwayayama-style." While the main chambers are similar in size, the corridor of Kotani Kofun is shorter. The stone chamber is also nearly identical in design to the {{ill|Minezuka Kofun|ja|峯塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minezuka Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]], suggesting a shift from local clan control of stonework to central administration under the imperial court during the terminal kofun periodKunihiko Kawakami, *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Fusosha, 2004, 11. 66-69.. Minezuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Reference: Bird's-eye view of the Minezuka Kofun stone chamber Kotani Kofun, genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the rear wall) Kotani Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Carved house-shaped sarcophagus inside the main chamber Kotani Kofun, genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Kotani Kofun, sendou-2-2.jpg|Entrance corridor (view toward the entrance) Kotani Kofun, sendou-1-2.jpg|Entrance corridor (view toward the main chamber) Kotani Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Entrance == Historic Site == === Nara Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Kotani Kofun: Designated on March 31, 1975 (Showa 50){{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf Nara Prefecture Cultural Property List]}} (Nara Prefectural Website).. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical site explanation board (installed by the Kashihara City Board of Education). * {{Cite book|year=1981|chapter=Kotani Kofun|title=Nara Prefecture Place names|series=[[Japanese Historical Place name Series]] 30|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Kotani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kotani Kofun}} * [https://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/kotani.html Kotani Kofun] - Kashihara City "Kashihara Visit Navi" {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小谷古墳|version=103773463|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:小谷古墳]] {{Commons category|Kotani Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q82961585}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

Minezuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Reference: Bird's-eye view of the Minezuka Kofun stone chamber Kotani Kofun, genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the rear wall) Kotani Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Carved house-shaped sarcophagus inside the main chamber Kotani Kofun, genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Kotani Kofun, sendou-2-2.jpg|Entrance corridor (view toward the entrance) Kotani Kofun, sendou-1-2.jpg|Entrance corridor (view toward the main chamber) Kotani Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Entrance == Historic Site == === Nara Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Kotani Kofun: Designated on March 31, 1975 (Showa 50){{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf Nara Prefecture Cultural Property List]}} (Nara Prefectural Website).. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical site explanation board (installed by the Kashihara City Board of Education). * {{Cite book|year=1981|chapter=Kotani Kofun|title=Nara Prefecture Place names|series=[[Japanese Historical Place name Series]] 30|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Kotani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kotani Kofun}} * [https://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/kotani.html Kotani Kofun] - Kashihara City "Kashihara Visit Navi" {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小谷古墳|version=103773463|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:小谷古墳]] {{Commons category|Kotani Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q82961585}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

[[commons:Category:Kotani Kofun]][[ja:小谷古墳]] [[commons:Category:Kotani Kofun]][[ja:小谷古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kotani Kofun]][[ja:小谷古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kotani Kofun | image = [[File:Kotani Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = | part_of = | location = {{ill|Kashihara|WD=Q496319}} | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 28 | coordinates = 29.03 | coordinates = 135 | coordinates = 47 | coordinates = 5.52 | omitted = JP-29 | type = Unknown | area = Length: 30 m
Height: 8 m | burial = Double-winged [[yokoana-style stone chamber]] (Iwayayama style)
(Contains a hewn [[House-shaped Sarcophagus|House-shaped sarcophagus]]) | Excavated Items = | Satellite Tombs = | built = [[:en:7th century|7th century]] | Buried Figure = | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = Designated historic site of Nara Prefecture: "Kotani Kofun" | Tangible Cultural Property = | notes = | map = Japan Nara | type = | map_dot_label = Kotani Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kotani Kofun''' (小谷古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is designation1 as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == The Kotani Kofun is situated on the southern slope of a ridge extending northeast from Kaibukiyama at the southern edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]Historic Site Explanation Board.. The mound is part of a cluster of eight kofun in the area, positioned at the easternmost point of the group. The mound has suffered significant erosion, and no archaeological excavations have been conducted. The mound's type is uncertain but is hypothesized to be either circular or square. It measures approximately 30 meters in length and 8 meters in height. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with double sidewalls, opening to the south. The stone chamber is constructed with well-cut granite blocks and features a sophisticated design similar to the [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]] in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka]], making it an example of the "Iwayayama-style stone chamber." Within the chamber lies a {{ill|House-shaped Sarcophagus|lt=House-shaped Sarcophagus|lt=House-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|lt=House-shaped sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} carved from [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]'s Tatsuyama Stone (stratified hyaloclastite)Tatsuyama Stone has been identified as rhyolitic stratified hyaloclastite (water-cooled pyroclastic rock) based on recent studies. See [https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html Tatsuyama Stone (Hyōgo Prefectural Website)] for details.. The grave goods have not been confirmed due to the lack of excavation. The mound dates to the [[:en:7th century|7th century]] during the late [[Kofun period]]. It is recognized as one of the representative terminal period kofun in Nara Prefecture, featuring a stone chamber comparable in map_size to those used for royal tombs. While the identity of the interred is unknown, the burial is speculated to belong to an influential aristocratic family, possibly of royal lineage. In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was suggested to be the tomb of [[:en:Empress Saimei|Empress Saimei]]. The site was designationed as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site in [[:en:1975|1975]] (Showa 50). Currently, access to the interior of the stone chamber is restricted. Nearby is the Kotani East Kofun (whose stone chamber remains unopened), and it has been proposed that the two kofun form a twin Kofun pairKunihiko Kawakami, *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Fusosha, 2004, 11. 70-72.. == burial == [[File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird's-eye view of the stone chamber.}}]] [[File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Detailed layout of the stone chamber.}}]] [[File:Kotani Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the carved sarcophagus.}}]] The burial facility comprises a double sidewall corridor-style stone chamber, opening to the south. Its area are as follows: * '''Total length:''' Approximately11.6 meters * '''Main chamber:''' Length ~5 meters, Width ~2.8 meters, Height ~2.7 meters * '''Entrance corridor:''' Length ~6.45 meters, Width ~1.9 meters, Height ~1.8 meters The chamber is constructed from large, cut granite blocks with meticulous craftsmanship. Both the rear and side walls of the main chamber consist of two layers, and the seams are sealed with plaster. A single large stone serves as the ceiling. The main chamber floor is elevated one step above the corridor. Inside the main chamber lies a house-shaped sarcophagus carved from Tatsuyama Stone[https://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/kotani.html Kotani Kofun] (Kashihara City "Kashihara Visit Navi").. The lid, almost semicylindrical in type, lacks suspension lugs, indicating a newer style within house-shaped sarcophagi. The sarcophagus measures 2.4 meters in length, 1.16 meters in width, and 0.82 meters in height. The chamber shares features with the Iwayayama Kofun, classified as "Iwayayama-style." While the main chambers are similar in size, the corridor of Kotani Kofun is shorter. The stone chamber is also nearly identical in design to the {{ill|Minezuka Kofun|ja|峯塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minezuka Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]], suggesting a shift from local clan control of stonework to central administration under the imperial court during the terminal kofun periodKunihiko Kawakami, *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Fusosha, 2004, 11. 66-69.. Minezuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Reference: Bird's-eye view of the Minezuka Kofun stone chamber Kotani Kofun, genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the rear wall) Kotani Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Carved house-shaped sarcophagus inside the main chamber Kotani Kofun, genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Kotani Kofun, sendou-2-2.jpg|Entrance corridor (view toward the entrance) Kotani Kofun, sendou-1-2.jpg|Entrance corridor (view toward the main chamber) Kotani Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Entrance == Historic Site == === Nara Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Kotani Kofun: Designated on March 31, 1975 (Showa 50){{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf Nara Prefecture Cultural Property List]}} (Nara Prefectural Website).. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical site explanation board (installed by the Kashihara City Board of Education). * {{Cite book|year=1981|chapter=Kotani Kofun|title=Nara Prefecture Place names|series=[[Japanese Historical Place name Series]] 30|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Kotani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kotani Kofun}} * [https://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/kotani.html Kotani Kofun] - Kashihara City "Kashihara Visit Navi" {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小谷古墳|version=103773463|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:小谷古墳]] {{Commons category|Kotani Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q82961585}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

Height: 8 m | burial = Double-winged [[yokoana-style stone chamber]] (Iwayayama style)
(Contains a hewn [[House-shaped Sarcophagus|House-shaped sarcophagus]]) | Excavated Items = | Satellite Tombs = | built = {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century}} | Buried Figure = | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = Designated historic site of Nara Prefecture: "Kotani Kofun" | Tangible Cultural Property = | notes = | map = Japan Nara | type = | map_dot_label = Kotani Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kotani Kofun''' (小谷古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|en|Kashihara, Nara|WD=Q496319|lt=Kashihara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}. It is designation1 as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == The Kotani Kofun is situated on the southern slope of a ridge extending northeast from Kaibukiyama at the southern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|en|Nara Basin|WD=Q10942269|lt=Nara Basin}}Historic Site Explanation Board.. The mound is part of a cluster of eight kofun in the area, positioned at the easternmost point of the group. The mound has suffered significant erosion, and no archaeological excavations have been conducted. The mound's type is uncertain but is hypothesized to be either circular or square. It measures approximately 30 meters in length and 8 meters in height. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with double sidewalls, opening to the south. The stone chamber is constructed with well-cut granite blocks and features a sophisticated design similar to the {{ill|Iwayayama Kofun|en|Iwayayama Kofun|WD=Q11474389|lt=Iwayayama Kofun}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|en|Asuka, Nara|WD=Q752397|lt=Asuka}}, making it an example of the "Iwayayama-style stone chamber." Within the chamber lies a {{ill|House-shaped Sarcophagus|lt=House-shaped Sarcophagus|lt=House-shaped sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|lt=House-shaped sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} carved from {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}}'s Tatsuyama Stone (stratified hyaloclastite)Tatsuyama Stone has been identified as rhyolitic stratified hyaloclastite (water-cooled pyroclastic rock) based on recent studies. See [https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html Tatsuyama Stone (Hyōgo Prefectural Website)] for details.. The grave goods have not been confirmed due to the lack of excavation. The mound dates to the {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century}} during the late [[Kofun period]]. It is recognized as one of the representative terminal period kofun in Nara Prefecture, featuring a stone chamber comparable in map_size to those used for royal tombs. While the identity of the interred is unknown, the burial is speculated to belong to an influential aristocratic family, possibly of royal lineage. In the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, it was suggested to be the tomb of [[:en:Empress Saimei|Empress Saimei]]. The site was designationed as a Nara Prefectural Historic Site in {{ill|1975|en|1975|WD=Q2479|lt=1975}} (Showa 50). Currently, access to the interior of the stone chamber is restricted. Nearby is the Kotani East Kofun (whose stone chamber remains unopened), and it has been proposed that the two kofun form a twin Kofun pairKunihiko Kawakami, *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Fusosha, 2004, 11. 70-72.. == burial == [[File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird's-eye view of the stone chamber.}}]] [[File:Kotani Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Detailed layout of the stone chamber.}}]] [[File:Kotani Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the carved sarcophagus.}}]] The burial facility comprises a double sidewall corridor-style stone chamber, opening to the south. Its area are as follows: * '''Total length:''' Approximately11.6 meters * '''Main chamber:''' Length ~5 meters, Width ~2.8 meters, Height ~2.7 meters * '''Entrance corridor:''' Length ~6.45 meters, Width ~1.9 meters, Height ~1.8 meters The chamber is constructed from large, cut granite blocks with meticulous craftsmanship. Both the rear and side walls of the main chamber consist of two layers, and the seams are sealed with plaster. A single large stone serves as the ceiling. The main chamber floor is elevated one step above the corridor. Inside the main chamber lies a house-shaped sarcophagus carved from Tatsuyama Stone[https://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/kotani.html Kotani Kofun] (Kashihara City "Kashihara Visit Navi").. The lid, almost semicylindrical in type, lacks suspension lugs, indicating a newer style within house-shaped sarcophagi. The sarcophagus measures 2.4 meters in length, 1.16 meters in width, and 0.82 meters in height. The chamber shares features with the Iwayayama Kofun, classified as "Iwayayama-style." While the main chambers are similar in size, the corridor of Kotani Kofun is shorter. The stone chamber is also nearly identical in design to the {{ill|Minezuka Kofun|ja|峯塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minezuka Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|WD=Q502196|lt=Tenri}}, suggesting a shift from local clan control of stonework to central administration under the imperial court during the terminal kofun periodKunihiko Kawakami, *Asuka Excavation Stories*, Fusosha, 2004, 11. 66-69.. Minezuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Reference: Bird's-eye view of the Minezuka Kofun stone chamber Kotani Kofun, genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the rear wall) Kotani Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Carved house-shaped sarcophagus inside the main chamber Kotani Kofun, genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Kotani Kofun, sendou-2-2.jpg|Entrance corridor (view toward the entrance) Kotani Kofun, sendou-1-2.jpg|Entrance corridor (view toward the main chamber) Kotani Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Entrance == Historic Site == === Nara Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Kotani Kofun: Designated on March 31, 1975 (Showa 50){{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf Nara Prefecture Cultural Property List]}} (Nara Prefectural Website).. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical site explanation board (installed by the Kashihara City Board of Education). * {{Cite book|year=1981|chapter=Kotani Kofun|title=Nara Prefecture Place names|series=[[Japanese Historical Place name Series]] 30|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Kotani Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kotani Kofun}} * [https://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/kotani.html Kotani Kofun] - Kashihara City "Kashihara Visit Navi" {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小谷古墳|version=103773463|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:小谷古墳]] {{Commons category|Kotani Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q82961585}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
